Title: Workflow engine v2: dependencies & approvals
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-483-PLN-008-workflow-engine-v2-dependencies-approvals.md`

## What this builds

Advanced workflow engine features on top of the core planner: dependency-based auto-shift, gate approval workflows, and multi-instance dependency tracking. Enables the "live schedule" that reacts to changes.

### Instance Lifecycle (State Diagram)

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft: create_instance
    Draft --> Planned: generate_schedule
    Planned --> Active: start_first_task
    Active --> Blocked: gate_blocks
    Blocked --> Active: gate_approved
    Active --> AtRisk: risk_detected
    AtRisk --> Active: risk_resolved
    Active --> Completed: final_task_done
    Completed --> Archived: archive
    Draft --> Cancelled: cancel
    Planned --> Cancelled: cancel
    Active --> Cancelled: cancel
```

### Dependency Auto-Shift

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Task "Item delivery" end_date moved +2 days] --> B[Engine queries planner.dependencies]
    B --> C{Successor exists?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Calculate forward shift + lag]
    D --> E[Update successor start/end dates]
    E --> F{Successor has successors?}
    F -->|Yes| D
    F -->|No| G[Mark plan as AtRisk if deadline missed]
    C -->|No| H[No propagation]
    G --> I[Notify affected assignees]
```

### Engine Types (Class Diagram)

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class PlannerEngine {
        +buildSchedule(phases, params)
        +shiftTask(taskId, deltaDays, tasks, deps)
        +detectCycles(deps)
        +checkGate(instance, phase, tasks, assignments, userId)
        +resolveDependencies(taskId, deps)
        +getEffectivePermissions(userId, assignments, instanceId)
    }
    class Task { +id, +instanceId, +phaseId, +title, +startDate, +endDate, +status, +priority }
    class Dependency { +id, +fromTaskId, +toTaskId, +depType, +lagDays }
    class Instance { +id, +orgId, +workflowId, +entityType, +entityId, +status }
    PlannerEngine --> Task : manages
    PlannerEngine --> Dependency : resolves
    PlannerEngine --> Instance : gates
    Task "1" --> "*" Dependency : has
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Dependency auto-shift: changing a task's dates propagates to successors with lag/type awareness.
* B — Gate approval flow: phase gates check task completion + role hierarchy before advancing.
* C — Cycle detection at shift time prevents infinite loops.
* D — Permission matrix enforces 4-tier role model (owner/manager/contributor/viewer) on all mutations.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Mastra planner AI tools + CopilotKit HITL
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-482-PLN-007-mastra-planner-ai-tools-copilotkit-hitl.md`

## What this builds

AI-powered planning inside the CopilotKit chat sidebar. The Mastra `production-planner` agent provides tools for schedule generation, shift analysis, and gate checks — all requiring explicit human approval (HITL) before writing to the DB.

### AI Schedule Generation with HITL

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User asks: "Build a 5-week schedule for Summer Lookbook"] --> B[Mastra production-planner agent]
    B --> C[Tool: buildSchedule]
    C --> D[Read workflow template + deliverables]
    D --> E[Generate proposed tasks + dependencies]
    E --> F[Return draft to chat]
    F --> G{User approves?}
    G -->|Yes| H[Tool: commitSchedule]
    H --> I[Edge function: schedule-shoot-plan]
    I --> J[INSERT planner.tasks + dependencies]
    J --> K[Notify subscribers]
    G -->|No| L[User edits / cancels]
    L --> M[Agent revises draft]
    M --> F
```

### User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Producer schedules a 5-week shoot with AI
    section Create plan
      Open shoot schedule: 5: Producer
      Generate AI schedule: 4: Producer, AI Agent
      Review proposed timeline: 4: Producer
      Approve schedule: 5: Producer
    section Execute
      Invite team members: 4: Producer
      Monitor phase transitions: 4: Producer
      Resolve blocker: 3: Producer, Photographer
      Approve final delivery: 5: Client approver
    section Wrap
      Archive instance: 5: Producer
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Mastra `production-planner` agent has tools: `buildSchedule`, `shiftTask`, `resolveDependencies`, `checkGate`, `getEffectivePermissions`.
* B — All WRITE tools require CopilotKit HITL (human-in-the-loop) confirmation before executing.
* C — CopilotKit sidepanel shows draft timeline as an A2UI card before commit.
* D — Agent reads current schedule state via RLS (READ tools).
* E — Agent responds in natural language with date summaries, warnings, and explanations.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Notification rules + Cloudflare Queue fan-out
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-481-PLN-006-notification-rules-cloudflare-queue-fan-out.md`

## What this builds

Declarative notification rules engine that triggers email / SMS / push / in-app alerts on planner events (phase transitions, gate approvals, task assignments). Uses Cloudflare Queues for reliable fan-out delivery.

### Notification Fan-Out

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Task as planner.tasks UPDATE
    participant Edge as schedule-shoot-plan edge function
    participant PG as Postgres
    participant Queue as planner-notify Queue
    participant Worker as planner-notify Worker
    participant SES as Email provider
    participant FCM as Push provider

    Task->>Edge: phase transition detected
    Edge->>PG: INSERT public.notifications
    Edge->>PG: SELECT planner.notification_rules
    Edge->>Queue: enqueue notification job
    Queue->>Worker: deliver job
    Worker->>PG: fetch notification + user prefs
    Worker->>SES: send email
    Worker->>FCM: send push
    Worker->>PG: update delivery_status
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — `planner.notification_rules` table stores declarative rules: event_type, target_role, channel, template_ref, delay_minutes.
* B — On planner event INSERT, an edge function evaluates matching rules and enqueues notification jobs.
* C — Cloudflare Queue buffers and retries delivery (max 3 retries, backoff).
* D — Channels: in_app (via existing `public.notifications`), email, push.
* E — Users can opt out per channel via user preferences.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Real-time sync via Supabase + Cloudflare Durable Objects
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-480-PLN-005-real-time-sync.md`

## What this builds

Real-time synchronization for planner instances via **Supabase Realtime** (DB change streaming) + **Cloudflare Durable Objects** (presence/cursor sync). Multiple users see task updates and each other's cursor positions in under one second.

### Real-Time Update Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor U1 as User A (Producer)
    actor U2 as User B (Photographer)
    participant SPA as Next.js SPA
    participant RT as Supabase Realtime
    participant PG as Postgres
    participant DO as planner-coordinator DO
    participant GW as planner-gateway Worker

    U1->>SPA: Drag task to new date
    SPA->>PG: UPDATE planner.tasks (RLS)
    PG-->>RT: change event
    RT-->>SPA: push updated task
    SPA-->>U1: render new position
    RT-->>SPA: push updated task (User B)
    SPA-->>U2: render new position
    SPA->>GW: send cursor position
    GW->>DO: broadcast presence
    DO-->>GW: presence list
    GW-->>SPA: show "Maya is viewing Production"
```

### Cloudflare Edge Components

```mermaid
block-beta
    columns 3
    spa["Next.js SPA"]:3
    gateway["planner-gateway Worker"]
    do["planner-coordinator DO"]
    kv["planner-cache KV"]
    queue["planner-notify Queue"]
    notify["planner-notify Worker"]
    ai["planner-ai Worker"]
    spa --> gateway
    gateway --> do
    gateway --> queue
    queue --> notify
    spa --> kv
    spa --> ai
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Supabase Realtime broadcasts planner.tasks/events/assignments changes on channel `planner:<instance_id>`
* B — Cloudflare Durable Objects track per-instance presence (who is viewing which task)
* C — WebSocket upgrade via planner-gateway Worker
* D — Sub-1-second latency for task updates
* E — Presence indicators in the planner workspace header
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Role-based views + assignments
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-479-PLN-004-role-based-views-assignments.md`

## Goal

Add instance-level role assignments and permission-aware views so each user sees only what matters to them.

## Problem

* Everyone sees the same schedule tab, which leaks details and creates noise.
* There is no way to assign owners to phases or restrict gate approvals.

## User story

> As a retoucher, when I open a production plan, I see only retouching tasks and approvals relevant to my role so I can focus without being overwhelmed.

## Acceptance criteria

* A — `planner.assignments` links user to instance with role and permissions JSONB.
* B — Default permission sets per role; owners can customize.
* C — Invite flow by email/role from planner settings.
* D — Timeline/kanban/calendar hide tasks the user cannot see; "My tasks" filter available.
* E — `/app/planner/dashboard` shows personalized stats and upcoming strip.
* F — Approval gates visible only to matching `phase.required_role`.

## Out of scope

Real-time presence, notifications, AI assignment suggestions, workflow gate logic.

## Wiring plan

* `app/src/lib/planner/permissions.ts`
* `app/src/components/planner/RoleDashboard.tsx`
* `app/src/components/planner/InviteMemberDialog.tsx`
* `app/src/components/planner/PlannerSettings.tsx`
* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/planner/dashboard/page.tsx`
* `supabase/functions/planner-invite-member/index.ts`

## Verify

* `cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/planner/permissions.test.ts`
* Browser login as retoucher: only retouching tasks visible
* `npm run supabase:verify-rls`
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Hybrid timeline / kanban / calendar UI shell
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-478-PLN-003-hybrid-timeline-kanban-calendar-ui-shell.md`

## What this builds

A **hybrid Timeline / Kanban / Calendar** UI shell inside the planner workspace — the primary visual interface for managing shoot production plans. All three views share one data model.

The shell follows the 3-panel layout: NavSidebar (left), workspace + OperatorChatDock (center), IntelligencePanel (right).

### Planner UI Shell (Block Diagram)

```mermaid
block-beta
    columns 3
    nav["NavSidebar"]:1
    workspace["Workspace: Timeline | Kanban | Calendar"]:1
    intel["IntelligencePanel"]:1
    chat["OperatorChatDock"]:2
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Three view modes: Timeline (Gantt-style bars per task/phase), Kanban (status-column swimlanes), Calendar (month/week/day grid).
* B — View switcher in the workspace header; persisted per-instance in `planner.view_configs`.
* C — Timeline view: horizontal bars grouped by phase, dependency arrows, drag-to-shift.
* D — Kanban view: columns for `todo / in_progress / blocked / done / cancelled`, drag-to-move.
* E — Calendar view: tasks as events on a month/week/day grid, drag-to-reschedule.

### Component reuse

* `OperatorChatDock` (existing)
* `IntelligencePanel` (existing)
* `NavSidebar` (existing)
* New: `PlannerTimeline`, `PlannerKanban`, `PlannerCalendar` — swappable via `PlannerView` wrapper.

### Wireframe references

See Linear document "[IPI-478](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-478/hybrid-timeline-kanban-calendar-ui-shell) Wireframes — Timeline / Kanban / Calendar" for full ASCII wireframes of each view mode, spec tables, and responsive behavior.

### Verify

* `npm run dev` → `/app/shoots/[id]/schedule` loads Timeline by default
* All three view modes render without error
* View switch persists in `view_configs`

Title: Shoot production timeline template
Description:
**Spec:** `linear/issues/IPI-477-PLN-002-shoot-production-timeline-template.md`

## What this builds

A reusable **5-Week Product Shoot** workflow template that generates the full phase/task timeline when a shoot is created or opened — matching the SquareShot production pattern.

### 5-Week Timeline (Gantt)

```mermaid
gantt
    title 5-Week Product Shoot Timeline
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    axisFormat %b %d
    section Pre-Production
    Brief confirmation      :done, a1, 2026-08-01, 3d
    Casting                 :active, a2, after a1, 4d
    Soft hold on shoot date :a3, after a2, 2d
    Item delivery           :a4, after a3, 4d
    Outfit confirmation     :a5, after a4, 3d
    section Logistics
    Payment & Scheduling    :a6, after a5, 3d
    Awaiting shoot          :a7, after a6, 3d
    section Production
    Production              :crit, a8, after a7, 4d
    section Post-Production
    Retouching              :a9, after a8, 3d
    Final approval          :a10, after a9, 2d
    Product return          :a11, after a10, 2d
```

### Acceptance criteria

* A — Workflow template "5-Week Product Shoot" with phases: Brief confirmation, Casting, Soft hold on shoot date, Item delivery, Outfit confirmation, Payment & Scheduling, Awaiting shoot, Production, Retouching, Final approval, Product return.
* B — Default business-day durations for each phase.
* C — Opening `/app/shoots/[id]/schedule` ensures a planner instance exists and generates tasks.
* D — Idempotent: no duplicate tasks on re-open.
* E — Existing shoots can be backfilled via script.

### Out of scope

Drag/edit UI, dependencies, AI generation, real-time sync.

### Wiring plan

* `supabase/functions/seed-shoot-plan/index.ts`
* `app/src/lib/planner/templates/shoot-5-week.ts`
* `supabase/seed-planner-workflows.sql`
* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/[id]/schedule/page.tsx`

### Verify

* `npm run supabase:push` + seed script
* `npx supabase functions invoke seed-shoot-plan --linked`
* `npm run supabase:verify-rls`

Title: BE-ST1 · Storage buckets
Description:
## BE-ST1 — Storage buckets (operator uploads)

**Track:** Backend · **Priority:** P2 · **Complexity:** S · **SSOT:** `tasks/backend/BE-ST1-storage-buckets.md`
**Branch:** `ipi/st1-operator-storage-buckets` · **Unblocks:** Asset upload (after [IPI-248](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-248/design-057-asset-library-react-parity-workspace) read-only)
**Related:** [IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery) (Cloudinary media pipeline)

## Objective

Configure Supabase Storage buckets with RLS policies. Remote has **0 buckets** (MCP 2026-07-06). Primary media remains Cloudinary — this bucket is for operator uploads/staging.

Title: DESIGN-081 · De-fork ApprovalCard — Consolidate 4 Implementations Into 1
Description:
## Context

Component-spec diff (2026-07-01, comparing `Universal design prompt/components/ApprovalCard.dc.html` against production) found that `ApprovalCard` — which the design system explicitly documents as "the only gate for an AI write action... do not fork" — has been **forked into 4 separate React implementations**:

* `app/src/components/brand-hub/approval-card.tsx`
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/BudgetApprovalCard.tsx`
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/DeliverableApprovalCard.tsx`
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/ShotListApprovalCard.tsx`

This is a rule violation already shipped to production, not just an unbuilt design gap — flagged as the single highest-priority finding from the component audit.

## Problem

Four independent ApprovalCard implementations mean HITL (human-in-the-loop) approval behavior can drift out of sync across Brand Hub vs the three Shoot HITL surfaces — a bug fix or design change to "how approvals work" now has to be applied 4 times, and already may have already drifted (prop names differ: source's `state`/`beforeText`/`afterText` vs production's `variant`/`before`/`after`).

## Proposal

Consolidate into one shared `ApprovalCard` component (props matching or reconciled with the design source: `title`, `confidence`, `evidence`, `before`, `after`, `variant`) that `brand-hub`, `BudgetApprovalCard`, `DeliverableApprovalCard`, and `ShotListApprovalCard` all render through — each surface passes its own data/copy into the shared component rather than maintaining its own markup/logic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] One `ApprovalCard` component exists under a shared location (e.g. `app/src/components/approval-card/`)
- [ ] Brand Hub's approval card, and all 3 shoot HITL cards (Budget/Deliverable/ShotList), render through the shared component
- [ ] No visual regression on any of the 4 surfaces
- [ ] Prop names reconciled with the design source (`variant`, `before`, `after`, `confidence`, `evidence`) — document the final agreed prop names in `tasks/design-docs/handoff/03-component-map.md`
- [ ] Existing tests for all 4 surfaces still pass; add one shared component test

## Skills

`vercel-react-best-practices` — `rerender-memo` (the shared component will render on every list of approvals; memoize it), `bundle-analyzable-paths` · `react-patterns` (Server Component vs Client Component boundary — approval actions likely need client interactivity, keep the boundary tight)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `Universal design prompt/components/ApprovalCard.dc.html` + `components/COMPONENTS.md`
* Component-map doc: `tasks/design-docs/handoff/03-component-map.md` (update after reconciling prop names)

## Out of scope

Changing HITL approval *behavior* — this is a structural consolidation, not a UX change. Any of the 4 surfaces' current visual variants should be preservable as props/variants on the shared component.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Intelligence Panel: AI Suggestion Rail
Description:
# IPI-285: Intelligence Panel — AI Suggestion Rail

**Linear:** [IPI-285](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-285)  
**Design Ref:** `Universal design prompt/Brand Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html` § Intelligence Panel  
**Parent:** IPI-255 (DESIGN-071 deferred items)  
**Priority:** High (P2)

---

## Context

IPI-255 shipped DNA scores and pending approvals in the Intelligence Panel. AI-powered suggestions were deferred as part of the original DESIGN-071 scope.

---

## Problem

Users must ask the AI assistant for recommendations explicitly. The Intelligence Panel could proactively surface AI-generated suggestions based on brand context, reducing cognitive load and surfacing non-obvious actions.

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Brand Manager seeing low commerce score

**As a** Brand Manager  
**I want** to see AI suggestions when DNA scores reveal gaps  
**So that** I know the next action to improve the score without asking

**Acceptance:** Suggestion "Add product shots to improve commerce readiness" appears when score < 70

### Story 2: Operator onboarding new brand

**As an** Operator  
**I want** proactive AI recommendations during brand analysis  
**So that** I can guide the brand owner on priority improvements

**Acceptance:** 3-5 suggestions render below DNA scores with action buttons

### Story 3: Creative Director with high consistency

**As a** Creative Director  
**I want** positive reinforcement when brand scores are strong  
**So that** I know to reference this brand for others

**Acceptance:** Suggestion "High consistency score — use as reference" appears when consistency > 85

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Operator reviews Beta Brand with low commerce score
    section Intelligence Panel Loads
      Navigate to /app/brand/beta: 5: Operator
      DNA scores load: Visual 80, Commerce 55: 3: Operator
    section AI Suggestion Appears
      See suggestion "Commerce readiness low": 4: Operator
      Read description "Add product shots": 5: Operator
      See confidence 85%: 5: Operator
    section Take Action
      Click "Plan shoot →" action button: 5: Operator
      Navigate to shoot wizard: 5: Operator
      Create product shoot with deliverables: 5: Operator
```

---

## Proposal

Add a SuggestionRail component to the Intelligence Panel that shows 3-5 AI-generated suggestions based on:

* Brand DNA scores (e.g., "Visual inconsistency detected → audit logo usage")
* Pending approvals status
* Current route context (brand detail vs shoot planning)
* Historical patterns

Implement `GET /api/brands/[id]/suggestions` endpoint backed by Mastra `brand-suggestions` tool.

---

## API Wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Returns | Agent |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `GET /api/brands/[id]/suggestions` | 🔴 create | `withOperatorAuth` + `createSupabaseServerClient` | `{ suggestions: Suggestion[] }` | `brand-intelligence` |

### Request

```typescript
GET /api/brands/[id]/suggestions
```

### Response

```typescript
{
  suggestions: [
    {
      id: string;
      type: 'action' | 'insight' | 'warning';
      title: string;
      description: string;
      action?: {
        label: string;
        href: string;
      };
      confidence: number; // 0-1
    }
  ]
}
```

### Mastra Tool Integration

```typescript
// app/src/mastra/tools/brand-suggestions.ts
import { createTool } from "@mastra/core/tools";  // ✅ CORRECT import path
import { z } from "zod";

export const brandSuggestionsTool = createTool({
  id: "generate-brand-suggestions",
  description: "Generate AI suggestions for brand based on DNA scores and context",
  inputSchema: z.object({
    brandId: z.string().uuid(),
    scores: z.object({
      visual: z.number(),
      audience: z.number(),
      consistency: z.number(),
      commerce_readiness: z.number(),
    }),
    context: z.enum(["brand-detail", "shoot-planning", "campaign"]).optional(),
  }),
  execute: async ({ context, brandId, scores }) => {
    // Generate 3-5 suggestions via Gemini
    // Return structured suggestion objects
  },
});
```

---

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as IntelligencePanel
    participant H as useIntelligencePanel
    participant A as GET /api/brands/[id]/suggestions
    participant M as Mastra brandIntelligenceAgent
    participant G as Gemini
    participant S as Supabase

    U->>H: mount with activeBrandId
    H->>A: fetch(`/api/brands/[id]/suggestions`)
    A->>A: withOperatorAuth(req)
    A->>S: SELECT scores FROM brand_scores WHERE brand_id = $1
    S-->>A: { visual: 80, audience: 70, ... }
    A->>M: brandSuggestionsTool.execute({ brandId, scores })
    M->>G: generateText with scores + context prompt
    G-->>M: [{ type, title, description, action }]
    M-->>A: { suggestions: [...] }
    A-->>H: { suggestions: [...] }
    H-->>U: update state.data.suggestions
    U->>U: render SuggestionRail
    
    Note over U: On suggestion click
    U->>U: navigate to suggestion.action.href
```

---

## Component Tree

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    IP[IntelligencePanel] --> SR[SuggestionRail]
    IP --> DNS[DnaScoresSection]
    IP --> AG[AssetsGrid]
    IP --> AS[ApprovalsSection]
    
    SR --> SG1[SuggestionCard]
    SR --> SG2[SuggestionCard]
    SR --> SG3[SuggestionCard]
    SR --> EMP[EmptyState]
    
    SG1 --> ICO[Icon]
    SG1 --> ACT[ActionButton]
```

---

## State Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Success: suggestions generated
    Loading --> Error: generation failed
    Success --> Empty: suggestions.length === 0
    Success --> Rail: suggestions.length > 0
    Rail --> Loading: brandId changes
    Empty --> Loading: brandId changes
    Error --> Loading: retry
```

---

## Wireframe

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Intelligence Panel                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ DNA Scores                                      │
│ ┌─────┬─────┬─────┬─────┐                      │
│ │ 75  │ V:80│ A:70│ C:90│                      │
│ └─────┴─────┴─────┴─────┘                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Suggestions (3)                                 │
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│ │💡 Visual inconsistency detected             ││
│ │   Audit logo usage across assets            ││
│ │   [Review assets →]                 85%     ││
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│ │⚠️  Commerce readiness low                   ││
│ │   Add product shots to improve score        ││
│ │   [Plan shoot →]                    60%     ││
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│ │✨ High consistency score                    ││
│ │   Use as reference for new brands           ││
│ │                                     90%     ││
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Assets (6)                                      │
│ ...                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Files to Create/Modify

### New Files

* `app/src/app/api/brands/[id]/suggestions/route.ts` — API route handler
* `app/src/app/api/brands/[id]/suggestions/route.test.ts` — API route tests
* `app/src/mastra/tools/brand-suggestions.ts` — Mastra suggestion tool
* `app/src/mastra/tools/brand-suggestions.test.ts` — Tool tests
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/suggestion-rail.tsx` — Rail component
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/suggestion-rail.test.tsx` — Component tests
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/suggestion-card.tsx` — Single suggestion

### Modified Files

* `app/src/lib/intelligence/panel-contract.ts` — Add `suggestions?: Suggestion[]` to `IntelligencePanelData`
* `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/intelligence-panel.tsx` — Render `SuggestionRail` when `data.suggestions` present
* `app/src/mastra/index.ts` — Register `brandSuggestionsTool` with `brandIntelligenceAgent`

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### A. Mastra Tool — `brand-suggestions`

- [ ] Tool accepts `brandId`, `scores`, optional `context`
- [ ] Generates 3-5 structured suggestions via Gemini
- [ ] Suggestions match schema (type, title, description, action, confidence)
- [ ] Confidence score 0-1 based on DNA score severity
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- brand-suggestions` → 4 tests passing

### B. API Route — `GET /api/brands/[id]/suggestions`

- [ ] Returns 401 when unauthenticated
- [ ] Returns 400 for invalid brand ID (non-UUID)
- [ ] Returns 403 when user lacks access to brand (RLS enforced)
- [ ] Fetches brand scores from Supabase
- [ ] Calls `brandSuggestionsTool.execute()` with scores
- [ ] Returns suggestions array
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- api/brands/[id]/suggestions` → 5 tests passing

### C. Component — SuggestionRail

- [ ] Renders 3-5 suggestion cards in vertical stack
- [ ] Shows empty state when `suggestions.length === 0`
- [ ] Shows loading state while generating
- [ ] Clicking action button navigates to `action.href`
- [ ] Confidence badge shows percentage (rounded)
- [ ] Icon matches suggestion type (💡 action, ✨ insight, ⚠️ warning)
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- suggestion-rail` → 6 tests passing

### D. Integration — IntelligencePanel

- [ ] `useIntelligencePanel` fetches suggestions when brandId present
- [ ] SuggestionRail renders below DNA scores, above Assets
- [ ] Rail does not render when `data.suggestions` is null/undefined
- [ ] No performance regression (polling still 30s)
- [ ] Test: `npm test -- intelligence-panel` → existing + new tests passing

### E. Test Coverage

- [ ] Mastra tool: 4 tests (input validation, generation, confidence, error handling)
- [ ] API route: 5 tests (auth, validation, RLS, success, error)
- [ ] SuggestionRail component: 6 tests (render, empty, loading, click, confidence, icons)
- [ ] Integration: Intelligence Panel renders suggestions correctly
- [ ] Full suite: `npm test` → no new failures

---

## Verification

```bash
cd app

# Mastra tool tests
npm test -- brand-suggestions

# API route tests
npm test -- api/brands

# Component tests
npm test -- suggestion-rail

# Intelligence Panel integration
npm test -- intelligence-panel

# Full suite
npm test

# Typecheck
npx tsc --noEmit

# Manual test
npm run dev
# Visit http://localhost:3002/app/brand/[id]
# Check Intelligence Panel for suggestion rail
# Click action buttons → verify navigation
```

---

## Out of Scope

* Suggestion dismissal/feedback mechanism
* Personalized suggestions based on user role
* Suggestion priority ranking beyond basic relevance
* Suggestion history/tracking
* User-submitted suggestions
* Suggestion voting/rating

---

## Dependencies

* IPI-255 ✅ (base Intelligence Panel)
* IPI-247 ✅ (route-agent map for context)
* Supabase `brand_scores` table ✅ (already exists)
* Mastra `brandIntelligenceAgent` ✅ (already exists)

---

## Skills Required

| Skill | Purpose |
| -- | -- |
| `/mastra` | Mastra tool creation, agent integration, Gemini prompts |
| `/ipix-supabase` | Query brand_scores for suggestion input |
| `/mermaid-diagrams` | Sequence diagrams for AI flow |
| `/ipix-wireframe` | SuggestionRail layout |
| `/copilotkit` | Ensure no conflicts with CopilotKit sidebar |
| `/shadcn` | Badge, Card components for suggestion UI |
| `/gen-test` | Mastra tool tests + component tests |

**Load order:** `mastra` → tool creation → `ipix-supabase` → data fetch → `mermaid-diagrams` → implement

---

## Design Reference

**File:** `Universal design prompt/Brand Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html`

**Context:** Intelligence Panel should proactively surface AI insights rather than waiting for user prompts. Suggestions provide actionable next steps based on brand DNA analysis.

**UX Principle:** "AI drafts, humans decide" — suggestions are recommendations, not automatic actions.

**Pattern:** Proactive intelligence > reactive chat. The panel guides users to high-value actions without explicit prompts.

Title: MI-03a · Preview redesign — pick a platform first, then its tabs
Description:
Upgrade the Channel Preview studio (`/app/preview`, shipped in MI-03 / IPI-188) from a fixed 4-frame wall to a **platform-first** chooser.

## Goal

User picks **platforms**, not individual placements. Each selected platform auto-renders all its placements, grouped under a tab. Fewer clicks, cleaner page.

## Scope (UI only — no new tables, no edge functions)

* **Platform multi-select** — checkboxes: Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Pinterest, YouTube, Amazon, Shopify (the 9 channels already seeded in `recommendation_rules`).
* **Auto-expand placements** — selecting Instagram shows Feed + Story + Reel; Facebook shows Feed + Story; etc.
* **Group by platform tabs** — `Instagram (3)`, `Facebook (2)`… instead of one long scroll.
* **Expand channel set 4 → 9** — extend `PREVIEW_CHANNELS`, `CHANNEL_LABELS`, `CHANNEL_LAYOUT`, `CHANNEL_FALLBACK_RATIO` in `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.ts`; extend `getAllChannelSpecs` to resolve all 9.
* **Status badge** — add an "Approved / Needs review" chip next to the existing spec caption (spec line `1080×1350 · 4:5 · JPEG` already renders — keep it).
* **Safe-zone toggle split** — current single "Show safe zones" checkbox → Safe Area / Crop / Text Area (cosmetic; reuses existing `safeZone` data).

## Files

* `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.ts` — extend constants to 9 channels
* `app/src/lib/media/channel-specs.server.ts` — `getAllChannelSpecs` over 9
* `app/src/components/media/channel-preview-studio.tsx` — selector + tabs
* `app/src/components/media/device-frame-preview.tsx` — Reel layout (reuse fullscreen), Pinterest/YouTube frames per fidelity decision (see MI-03r note: generic frame first)

## Out of scope (separate tasks)

AI review (MI-03b), approval persistence (MI-03c), campaign view (MI-03d), publishing (MI-03e), smart cropping (MI-03r). Bespoke YouTube/Pinterest chrome can start as a generic ratio+safe-zone frame.

## Acceptance

* Select platforms → only those render, grouped in tabs
* Instagram selection shows Feed + Story + Reel
* All 9 seeded channels resolvable; unseeded → graceful "no spec" fallback
* One concern per PR (UI only), passes `npm run lint` + `tsc --noEmit` + `build`

Title: SCR-21b · Booking Wizard draft persistence
Description:
## IPI-410D — Booking Wizard Draft Persistence

**Priority:** P3 · **Track:** Frontend UX
**Depends on:** [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard) (Booking Wizard — Done)

## Purpose

Preserve Booking Wizard progress across page refresh and accidental tab close. Currently all wizard state is in React useState — any navigation loss resets to step 0.

## Current state

`booking-wizard-workspace.tsx` stores all state in React `useState` hooks (step, dateStart, dateEnd, rateQuotedInput, messageDraft, draftFetched, etc.). There is no localStorage/sessionStorage backup, no Supabase draft table, no recovery mechanism.

## Deliverables

- [ ] sessionStorage MVP: persist { step, dateStart, dateEnd, rate, message, draftFetched } on field changes
- [ ] Recovery banner on reload: "You have a draft booking request. Resume where you left off?"
- [ ] "Start over" clears saved draft
- [ ] Successful send clears saved draft
- [ ] Reject/Cancel clears saved draft
- [ ] Expiration strategy: discard saved drafts older than 24h
- [ ] Multi-tab safety: last-write-wins is acceptable for MVP (no conflict resolution)
- [ ] No sensitive data stored (only wizard fields — no auth tokens, no booking IDs from prior requests)
- [ ] Tests: reload recovery, draft clear on send, draft clear on start-over, stale draft discard

## Future integration: Planner

Once the Planner ([IPI-476](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-476/planner-schema-and-reusable-engine-core)+) is live, a persisted draft that was recovered and sent would feed into:

```
Booking Approved → Planner Instance → Tasks → Notifications → Calendar → Shoot Timeline
```

Draft persistence ensures the operator doesn't lose context between wizard completion and Planner task creation. If the draft survives to send, the planner can pick up the booking event via notification/trigger.

## Out of scope

* Supabase draft storage table (not needed for MVP — sessionStorage suffices)
* Cross-device draft sync
* Conflict resolution for multi-tab edits
* Booking agent draft recovery (agent memory is ephemeral per process)

## Proof

```bash
cd app && npx vitest run src/components/booking/
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A[Navigate to /app/model or /app/roster] --> B[Role gate check]
  B -->|Talent role| C[Model dashboard]
  B -->|Agency role| D[Roster dashboard]
  C --> C1[KPI: shoot count, pending approvals]

```

| KPI bar | Upcoming shoots, pending approvals, next booking | Total talent, upcoming shoots, pending availability |
| Upcoming bookings | My bookings list | All talent bookings |
| Quick actions | View profile, check availability | Add talent, manage roster |

## Route & Grid

* **Routes:** `/app/model` + `/app/roster` (both greenfield — create)
* **Shell:** fixed-3col, grid `56px | minmax(0,1fr) | 340px`

| talent_profiles table | 🟡 verify |
| get_talent_profile | 🟢 live |
| KPI aggregation | 🟡 needs RPC or client-side count |
| Upcoming shoots query | 🟡 needs or join bookings + shoots |

## States

| State | AC |

| Populated (model) | KPI bar 3-col, upcoming bookings, quick actions |
| Populated (roster) | Talent list, upcoming shoots, add talent CTA |

Title: BE-ACT1 · Org activity log + list_org_activity RPC
Description:
## BE-ACT1 — Org activity log + list_org_activity RPC

**Track:** Backend · **Priority:** P3 · **Complexity:** M · **SSOT:** `tasks/backend/BE-ACT1-org-activity-log.md`
**Branch:** `ipi/act1-org-activity-log` · **Unblocks:** SCR-18 ([IPI-408](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-408/scr-18-collaboration-activity-audit))

## Objective

Org-scoped append-only activity feed for `/app/activity` (SCR-18). **New** `org_activity_events` **table** + `list_org_activity` **RPC** federating `crm_activities` + `agent_decision_log` into one cursor-paginated feed.

## Gap analysis

| Source | Live | Use |
| -- | -- | -- |
| `org_activity_events` | 🔴 | Native comments, mentions, edits, approvals, system |
| `crm_activities` | 🟢 | Map to comment/edit category |
| `agent_decision_log` | 🟢 | Map to approval category |
| `list_org_activity` RPC | 🔴 | Single query for React |

## Design decision

| Option | Verdict |
| -- | -- |
| Extend `crm_activities.type` | ❌ Breaks CRM anchor + semantics |
| Federation-only view (no new table) | ❌ Can't store open/resolved comments or @mentions |
| `org_activity_events` **+ federation RPC** | ✅ Matches SCR-18 DC filters + immutable audit |

## Proposed schema

```sql
create table public.org_activity_events (
  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id        uuid not null references public.organizations(id) on delete cascade,
  actor_id      uuid references public.profiles(id) on delete set null,
  event_type    text not null check (event_type in ('comment','mention','approval','edit','upload','system')),
  entity_type   text not null,      -- brand, shoot, campaign, asset, booking, deal
  entity_id     uuid,
  summary       text not null,      -- "edited the brief for Adidas Q2 campaign"
  body          text,               -- comment text / diff snippet
  metadata      jsonb not null default '{}',
  status        text not null default 'open' check (status in ('open','resolved','na')),
  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
);
```

Append-only. No `updated_at`. `na` for non-actionable rows. `resolve_org_activity_event` RPC for open→resolved.

Indexes: `(org_id, created_at desc)`, `(org_id, event_type)`, `(org_id, status)` where `status='open'`.

## RLS

| Policy | Rule |
| -- | -- |
| SELECT | `is_org_member(org_id)` |
| INSERT | `is_org_member(org_id)` and `actor_id = auth.uid()` |
| UPDATE | none (use RPC) |
| DELETE | none (append-only) |

### `list_org_activity(p_org_id, p_filter, p_scope, p_cursor, p_limit)`

UNION ALL across:

1. `org_activity_events` — primary
2. `crm_activities` — map type→category
3. `agent_decision_log` — map→approval

Returns stable ActivityFeedRow shape.

### `resolve_org_activity_event(p_id, p_org_id)`

Sets status=resolved. Org-member gated.

## Files affected

* `supabase/migrations/*_org_activity_events.sql` (migration-only PR)
* App types regenerate

## Do NOT

* Ship triggers on every table in ACT1
* Ship React for SCR-18 in same PR
* Consulted: ipix-supabase, task-verifier

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Migration applied on remote
- [ ] RLS per `is_org_member` pattern
- [ ] `list_org_activity` returns federated rows
- [ ] `resolve_org_activity_event` works
- [ ] Indexes on filter columns
- [ ] `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls` green
- [ ] Types regenerated

## Verification

```bash
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify && npm run supabase:verify-rls
```

```sql
select to_regclass('public.org_activity_events');
select proname from pg_proc where proname in ('list_org_activity','resolve_org_activity_event');
```

## Design reference

* DC: `Pages/SCR-18-Collaboration-Audit.dc.html`
* Screen task: `tasks/screens/SCR-18-collaboration.md`
* Backend task: `tasks/backend/BE-ACT1-org-activity-log.md`

`ipix-supabase` · `task-verifier`

Title: MI-03d · See one photo across every platform at once
Description:
Flip the model: instead of previewing one placement at a time, upload one asset and see it across a whole campaign set at once — IG Feed/Story/Reel, Facebook, TikTok, Pinterest, Amazon, Shopify, (Email later).

## Scope

* A "Campaign" mode that takes one uploaded asset and lays out every target placement in a single board.
* Per-card status rolls up to a campaign-level readiness summary (uses MI-03b checks).

## Depends on

* MI-03a (selection + multi-placement rendering)
* Real value depends on MI-03r (smart per-placement cropping) — without it, every card shows the same CSS-cropped image rather than a properly recomposed one.

Blocked by MI-03a.
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MI-03c · Approve, edit, or schedule each image from the preview
Description:
Each preview card gets actions: ✅ Approve · ✏️ Edit · 📅 Schedule.

## Reuse, don't rebuild

A HITL approval-card pattern is already in flight (`feat/ipi-52-approval-cards`, `app/src/components/brand-hub/approval-card.tsx`) and the roadmap's MI-08 covers "recommendation HITL approval card → persist via edge fn". Adopt that pattern: nothing persists until Approve; write via a Supabase Edge Function (never write tables directly from the client).

## Scope

* Approve → persist an approval record (which asset, which placement, by whom) via edge fn.
* Edit → adjust caption/asset URL/crop and re-preview.
* Schedule → set a publish date (feeds MI-03e).

## Out of scope

🔄 Regenerate (generative image editing — capability doesn't exist yet).

Blocked by MI-03a. Coordinate with MI-08 / the approval-cards branch to share the HITL component.

## Context

`Universal design prompt/design-audit-2026-06-28-rev2.md` (finding #1, 🟡) flagged that the design system's own Rule 9 mandates Lucide icons, but production `app/src/components/operator-panel/nav-sidebar.tsx` renders raw emoji glyphs (⌂ 📷 ◈ 🖼 📣 🤝 💬) for Home/Shoots/Brand/Assets/Campaigns/Matching/Threads. Re-verified live on disk (2026-07-01): **still emoji, not fixed** — this is a real, currently-open gap, not a stale finding.

## Problem

Emoji render inconsistently across OS/browser font stacks (different weight, color rendering on some platforms since these are pictographic emoji, not outline glyphs) and violate the project's own icon-system rule.

## Proposal

Replace the 7 emoji glyphs in `nav-sidebar.tsx` with the equivalent `lucide-react` icon components (e.g. Home, Camera, Gem/Layers for Brand, Image, Megaphone, Handshake/Users for Matching, MessageSquare for Threads — pick the closest semantic match, confirm with design if ambiguous).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 7 nav icons use `lucide-react` components, no emoji glyphs remain in `nav-sidebar.tsx`
- [ ] Visual weight/size consistent with the rest of the nav (match existing icon sizing token)
- [ ] No layout shift — icon bounding box matches current emoji footprint
- [ ] Import icons directly (`import { Home } from "lucide-react"`), not a barrel/wildcard import

## Skills

`vercel-react-best-practices` — `bundle-barrel-imports`: import each Lucide icon directly rather than a barrel import, to avoid pulling the full icon set into the bundle

## Dependencies

None — isolated, self-contained change to one file

## Out of scope

Icon changes anywhere else in the app (scope this to NavSidebar only; expand in a follow-up if other emoji usage is found elsewhere)

Title: MI-03r · Research auto-cropping images per platform (Cloudinary)
Description:
**Later / research — do not build yet.**

Today the preview shows the *same* asset in every frame, cropped only by CSS `object-fit`. To truly turn one upload into correct outputs per placement (the "move image up 15%" / auto-recompose idea), we need real server-side cropping.

## Research questions

* Use Cloudinary transforms (`c_fill`, `g_face`/`g_auto`, per target aspect ratio + safe zone) to generate a correctly-cropped derivative per placement.
* Where do transforms run — on render (URL params) vs. baked derivatives stored in `cloudinary_assets` / `asset_variants`?
* How to respect each placement's safe zones from `image_specs` when choosing the crop.
* Cost/perf of generating N derivatives per asset per campaign.

## Why deferred

This is the advanced engine under Campaign view (MI-03d) and the "fix: move image up 15%" suggestions (MI-03b). Valuable but not needed for the platform-first UX win (MI-03a). Spike it, write findings, then size implementation.

Output of this task = a short findings doc + a sizing estimate, not production code.

Title: Planner schema & reusable engine core
Description:
**Audit score: 84/100** — Strong foundation, tightened via this revision.

## Goal

Introduce a single `planner` schema and pure TypeScript engine that can model any time-bound, multi-stakeholder workflow across shoots, campaigns, and CRM deals.

## Key changes per audit

* **Entity type safety:** `entity_type IN ('shoot', 'campaign', 'crm_deal')` CHECK constraint
* **No duplicate instances:** `UNIQUE (org_id, entity_type, entity_id, workflow_id)`
* **RLS role matrix:** four-tier (owner / manager / contributor / viewer) with explicit permissions
* **Status enums:** instance (`draft→planned→active→blocked→completed→archived→cancelled`), task (`todo→in_progress→blocked→done→cancelled`), dependency types
* **Cycle detection:** engine rejects circular deps (A→B→A)
* **Indexes:** 6 B-tree indexes on entity lookup, status, assignee, events, dependencies
* **Two-PR split:** PR1 = schema + RLS + seed; PR2 = engine + types + tests (prevents oversized ~250+ LOC migration)
* **Pure engine:** v1 engine is read-only schedule calc + permission resolution — no DB writes

## Acceptance criteria

* A — `planner` schema with 10 tables, enums, CHECK constraints, indexes.
* B — Polymorphic linking with entity type constraint + uniqueness.
* C — RLS with four-tier role matrix (owner/manager/contributor/viewer).
* D — Status enums for instances, tasks, dependencies.
* E — Pure TypeScript engine with `createInstance`, `buildSchedule`, `shiftTask`, `resolveDependencies` (with cycle detection), `checkGate`, `getEffectivePermissions`.
* F — Idempotent seed for default workflow template.
* G — Realtime publication for instances, tasks, events, assignments.

## Out of scope (v1 engine only — no writes)

Cloudflare DO/Queue, UI, AI, auto-shift, approval v2, DB-side business logic in engine.

## Wiring — two PRs

**PR 1:** `supabase/migrations/·sql` + `seed-planner-workflows.sql` + RLS verify probe
**PR 2:** `app/src/lib/planner/engine.ts` + `types.ts` + `engine.test.ts`

## Verify

* `npm run supabase:verify-rls` — all 4 role tiers
* `cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/planner/engine.test.ts` — ≥90% coverage incl cycle detection + constraint validation

## IPI-410A — Booking QA Seed Data & E2E Reliability

**Priority:** P2 · **Track:** Backend/QA · **Extends:** [IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) (BE-SD1)
**Depends on:** [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard) (Booking Wizard — Done), [IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) (DB seed data — Done)

## Purpose

Extend dev/QA seed data so the Booking Wizard can be tested end-to-end with real talent profiles, valid org memberships, availability, and booking request writes.

## Current state

[IPI-451](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-451/be-sd1-database-seed-data-for-developmenttesting) (BE-SD1) shipped `supabase/seed.sql` with deterministic UUIDs for orgs, brands, CRM — but it contains **0 talent profiles, 0 availability rows, 0 bookings**. The Booking Wizard has **22 Vitest unit tests** (component level) but **zero Playwright E2E tests**.

## Deliverables

- [ ] talent profile seed data (2+ profiles, mixed rate tiers, verified/different verification_status)
- [ ] talent availability seed data (available + overlapping date ranges)
- [ ] QA org membership linking seed users to seed org
- [ ] Playwright E2E: complete booking wizard happy path (dates → rate → message → send → success)
- [ ] Playwright E2E: invalid talent UUID shows not-found
- [ ] Playwright E2E: no write before final Send click (prove via DB assertion or mock)
- [ ] Seed data is deterministic (fixed UUIDs), idempotent (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
- [ ] No destructive writes to remote during E2E tests (test teardown or isolated test DB)

## Future integration: Planner

Booking approval triggers Planner ([IPI-476](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-476/planner-schema-and-reusable-engine-core)+):

```
Booking Approved → Planner Instance → Tasks → Notifications → Calendar → Shoot Timeline
```

This E2E seed data should include enough entities (talent → booking → planner path) so the Planner integration E2E can reuse these seeds without duplication. Coordinate seed UUIDs with [IPI-476](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-476/planner-schema-and-reusable-engine-core).

Title: SCR-31 · CRM Deal Detail — React Parity
Description:
## SCR-31 — CRM Deal Detail React Parity

**Priority:** P1 · **Dependencies:** [IPI-395](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-395/scr-30-crm-pipeline-react-parity) (SCR-30 Pipeline) — pipeline should be functional first; [IPI-244](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-244/intel-002-approvalqueue-hitl-write-actions-in-intelligencepanel) (ApprovalCard component)

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-31-crm-deal-detail.md`

**Route:** `/app/crm/pipeline/[id]`

**Design source:** `Pages/SCR-31-CRM-Deal-Detail.dc.html`

**Current state:** 30-line stub with id/org validation + CrmScreenGate

---

## Overview

Deal detail with stage selector, activity timeline, health score, approval gate for won/lost conversion, and brand-convert handoff path. Won/lost transition is gated behind an **ApprovalCard** — this is the only path that can set terminal stages.

## Purpose

Operator views and advances a deal through stages, sees activity history and health, and triggers won/lost conversion with HITL approval.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Pipeline kanban] -->|click deal card| B[Deal detail /app/crm/pipeline/[id]]
  B --> C[Header: deal name, value, company, stage chip]
  B --> D[Stage selector: 6 buttons, Lead→Lost]
  D --> E[Lead→Negotiation: immediate stage change]
  D --> F[Won/Lost: → ApprovalCard gate]
  F --> G[ApprovalCard: confirm with reason]
  G --> H[Won banner + brand convert link]
  G --> I[Lost: closed-lost state]
  B --> J[Activity timeline: deal events, AI summary, calls, emails]
  B --> K[IntelligencePanel: health score factors]
  B --> L[Chat dock: crm-assistant context]
```

## Component reuse verification

| Component | Usage | Reuse from |
| -- | -- | -- |
| StatusChip | Deal stage chip | RF-01 |
| ApprovalCard | Won/Lost gate confirmation | Existing component ([IPI-244](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-244/intel-002-approvalqueue-hitl-write-actions-in-intelligencepanel)) |
| EmptyState | No activity | RF-A7b |
| ErrorState | Error with retry | RF-A7b |
| IntelligencePanel | Health factors, NBA, drafted follow-up | Existing shell |
| N/A | NO new stage engine | Shared with pipeline (SCR-30) |
| N/A | NO custom timeline | Unified `crm_activities` pattern |

## Data source table

| Section | Source | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Deal header | `crm_deals` by id | 🟢 live |
| Company name | `crm_companies` join | 🟢 live |
| Primary contact | `crm_contacts` join via deal | 🟢 verify deal→contact FK |
| Value | `deals.value` | 🟢 live |
| Linked shoot | `deals.shoot_id` → `shoot.shoots` | 🟢 verify FK exists |
| Stage | `deals.stage` | 🟢 live |
| Activity timeline | `crm_activities` by deal_id | 🟢 live |
| Health factors | crm-assistant or derived | 🟡 verify agent |
| Brand conversion | Won deal → `brands` create/link | 🟡 verify brand pathway |
| Intel panel | crm-assistant | 🟢 wired |

## DC grid spec

| Property | DC value |
| -- | -- |
| Shell | `56px | minmax(0,1fr) | 320px` |
| Breadcrumb | Pipeline > {company} — {deal name} |
| Header | h1 22px/700 for deal name, mono 18px/600 for value, stage chip |
| Stage buttons | 6 pill buttons, colored by stage, won/lost=gated style |
| Overview grid | 2×2, 4 fields: Company, Contact, Value, Linked shoot |
| Activity timeline | Same unified pattern: icon circle + line + content |
| Won banner | green bg, check-circle icon, "Converted to brand" + brand link |
| Lost banner | red bg, alert |
| Intel panel | Health score bar chart, ApprovalCard for drafted follow-up |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | Deal loads by id — header shows name, value, company, stage chip | browser |
| 2 | Breadcrumb: Pipeline > {company} — {name} | click → list |
| 3 | Stage selector: 6 buttons, clicking Lead→Negotiation changes stage immediately | click updates |
| 4 | Won/Lost buttons trigger ApprovalCard overlay | click → confirmation |
| 5 | ApprovalCard: Cancel reverts, Approve transitions stage | both paths tested |
| 6 | Won state: green banner with "Converted to brand" + link | visual match to DC |
| 7 | Lost state: closed-lost indication | visual match |
| 8 | Overview grid: Company (↗ link), Contact (↗ link), Value (£), Linked shoot (↗ link) | all navigable |
| 9 | Activity timeline: AI summary, proposal sent, scoping call, deal created | visual match to DC |
| 10 | Health factors in IntelligencePanel: 3-bar chart (recency, velocity, engagement) | panel updates |
| 11 | ApprovalCard in Intel panel: AI-drafted follow-up with Edit/Approve | matches DC |
| 12 | Loading: skeleton | visible during fetch |
| 13 | Empty activity: honest empty state | no fake data |
| 14 | Error: ErrorState with retry | mock failure |
| 15 | Not found: invalid id → 404 | notFound() |
| 16 | tokens.css only — no legacy hex | grep check |
| 17 | CrmScreenGate removed | component replaced |

## Mobile & a11y

| # | Requirement |
| -- | -- |
| 1 | Stage selector wraps on mobile, buttons >=44px |
| 2 | Timeline keyboard-navigable |
| 3 | ApprovalCard focus-traps on open (Esc closes) |
| 4 | Stage/risk never color-only — text label present |
| 5 | 390px: stacked layout, no overflow |

## AI boundaries

| Area | Status |
| -- | -- |
| crm-assistant | 🟢 route context |
| Health score | 🟡 may use crm-assistant or derived |
| ApprovalCard | 🟢 existing component, reads agent decision log |
| Chat dock | 🟡 placeholder |
| AI writes | 🔴 none — won/lost gated, draft message is HITL |
| Stage transitions | 🟢 immediate for non-terminal, ApprovalCard for terminal |

## Approval gate rules (from DC, CRM-HANDOFF.md)

1. Clicking Won/Lost opens an ApprovalCard, does NOT immediately transition
2. ApprovalCard shows: current stage → target stage, deal summary, reason
3. "Cancel" closes the card. "Approve" transitions and (for Won) triggers brand-convert
4. Won deals show conversion banner + link to Brand Detail
5. No drag, button, or agent may bypass the approval gate (hard rule from CRM-HANDOFF.md §3)

## Test plan

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && CI=true npm run build
```

Browser: deal renders · stage selector works · won/lost approval gate · conversion banner · activity timeline · 390px pass · no console errors

## Out of scope

* Creating/editing deals inline
* Won→brand conversion automation (API exists but full flow is follow-up)
* Reports/analytics
* Email/calendar sync

Title: FIX · Production Error Boundaries for Operator Routes
Description:
## Problem

Zero `error.tsx` files exist in the `(operator)/` route group. Any unhandled error in an operator page (data fetch failure, Supabase RPC timeout, agent crash) renders a white screen with no recovery path. 17 routes affected.

## Context

The root `not-found.tsx` catches 404s but there is no error boundary at any level — not at the group level (`(operator)/error.tsx`), not at individual route level. The Next.js `error.tsx` convention wraps the page in an Error Boundary that receives `{error, reset}` props.

## Scope

Add a single `(operator)/error.tsx` that:

* Renders within the OperatorPanel shell (sidebar + CopilotKit still functional)
* Shows a clear error message
* Provides a "Try again" button (`reset()`)
* Provides a "Return to dashboard" link (`/app`)
* Logs the error to console for debugging
* Respects dark mode

## Out of scope

* Individual per-route error.tsx files (group-level catch-all is sufficient for MVP)
* Custom error reporting service (Sentry, etc.)
* Recovery from CopilotKit provider-level crashes

## Acceptance

- [ ] Navigating to any broken `/app/*` route shows error page, not white screen
- [ ] "Try again" button calls `reset()` to re-render
- [ ] "Return to dashboard" link navigates to `/app`
- [ ] Error is logged to console
- [ ] `npm run build` passes (no type errors)

## Files

**NEW:** `app/src/app/(operator)/error.tsx` — client component error boundary (~30 lines)

Title: Cloudinary Config Cleanup
Description:
Fix broken Cloudinary environment/config values before any SDK or upload work (IPI-257 074a) can safely proceed.

**Real-world example:** it's like a delivery address with an extra apartment number typo'd onto the end — the postal code alone still "looks" almost right, but the truck goes to the wrong building every time. That's what `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` is doing today.

## Tasks

- [ ] Fix corrupted `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` in `.env.local` — currently `dzqy2ixl0/nvdlhrodvevgwdsneplk` (has a stray `/nvdlhrodvevgwdsneplk` suffix appended), should be `dzqy2ixl0` (matches `CLOUDINARY_URL` and `NEXT_CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` already in the same file)
- [ ] Fix the hardcoded wrong cloud name in `dev-panel-fixture.ts` (currently `dza2bjwwp`, does not match the real project cloud name)
- [ ] Remove stale `VITE_CLOUDINARY_*` vars (Vite is retired) and any unused duplicate `NEXT_CLOUDINARY_*` vars
- [ ] Add missing `f_auto,q_auto` to any named transform preset that doesn't already end with it (see preset table in `docs/linear/issues/IPI-257-cloudinary-pipeline.md`)

## Why this is priority #1

Confirmed live bug as of 2026-07-03 — this breaks any code path reading `process.env.CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` directly before any SDK install or upload-signing work is even attempted.

Related: full detail in `tasks/design-docs/cloudinary.md` Phase 5 audit table and roadmap.

Title: BE-D1 · Campaigns schema migration (scope trimmed from original)
Description:
## BE-D1 — Campaigns schema migration

**Role:** You are implementing this as an iPix engineer. Stack: Next.js `app/`, Supabase remote-only, one concern per PR. `ipix-supabase` and `task-verifier` skills govern this work; load them before coding.

**Plain English:** Add `campaigns` + `campaign_deliverables` tables so the Campaigns UI (SCR-07) has data to render. Today no `public.campaigns` table exists — Campaigns route at `/app/campaigns` shows a `SectionPlaceholder` stub.

**Context — what's broken today:**

* Remote has **zero** campaign tables (MCP verified 2026-07-06)
* `crm_deals.campaign_id` column exists but is **orphaned** — no FK target
* Campaigns.v2.image-first.dc.html shows 4 statuses (`Planning`, `Active`, `Live`, `Complete`) with progress bars, date ranges, and deliverable checklists — none of which can be stored
* Creative Director agent cannot reference campaign context

**Mistake we prevent:** Do NOT add campaign data to an existing table. Do NOT create a non-org-scoped table. Do NOT skip RLS — every table in `public` gets org-scoped RLS.

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/backend/BE-D1-campaigns-schema-IPI-268.md`

**Blocked by:** [IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery) (Cloudinary Media Pipeline — optional, FK to cover images)
**Unblocks:** [IPI-249](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-249/design-058-campaign-management-react-parity-workspace) (SCR-07 Campaigns UI), [IPI-297](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-297/design-091-campaign-performance-react-port-drill-down) (SCR-17 Campaign Performance), [IPI-399](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-399/be-d2-analytics-views-rpcs) (BE-D2 Analytics views), crm_deals.campaign_id FK

**Skills:** `ipix-supabase` · `task-verifier`

---

## User stories

> As an **Operator**, when I open Campaigns, I see a list of campaigns with name, status, date range, and progress so I can understand what's running.

> As an **Operator**, when I click a campaign, I see its brand context, deliverables checklist, and timeline so I can manage production without switching tools.

> As a **Creative Director agent**, when a user asks "What campaigns are active for Brand X?", I can query `campaigns` + `campaign_deliverables` so I give accurate answers.

---

## Migration SQL

### Helper function: `set_updated_at()`

```sql
create or replace function public.set_updated_at()
returns trigger as $$
begin
  new.updated_at = now();
  return new;
end;
$$ language plpgsql;
```

### Helper function: `check_campaign_org_consistency()`

```sql
create or replace function public.check_campaign_org_consistency()
returns trigger as $$
begin
  if new.org_id != (select org_id from public.brands where id = new.brand_id) then
    raise exception 'campaign.org_id (%) must match brand.org_id (%)', new.org_id, (select org_id from public.brands where id = new.brand_id);
  end if;
  return new;
end;
$$ language plpgsql;
```

### Type: `campaign_status`

```sql
create type public.campaign_status as enum ('planning', 'active', 'live', 'complete');
```

Matches DC design: Planning → Active → Live → Complete.

### Type: `deliverable_status`

```sql
create type public.deliverable_status as enum ('pending', 'in_progress', 'review', 'approved');
```

### Note on `campaign_objective_type`

Already exists on remote — do not recreate:

```
campaign_objective_type: brand_awareness, product_launch, conversion, retention, community, seo_discovery, ecommerce_direct
```

### Table: `campaigns`

```sql
create table public.campaigns (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id      uuid not null references public.organizations(id) on delete cascade,
  brand_id    uuid not null references public.brands(id) on delete cascade,
  name        text not null,
  status      public.campaign_status not null default 'planning',
  objective   public.campaign_objective_type,
  start_date  date,
  end_date    date,
  cover_url   text,                          -- Cloudinary URL, set via RPC not direct UPDATE
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);
```

### Table: `campaign_deliverables`

```sql
create table public.campaign_deliverables (
  id          uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  campaign_id uuid not null references public.campaigns(id) on delete cascade,
  phase       smallint not null check (phase between 1 and 12),
  label       text not null,                                      -- e.g. "Brief", "Moodboard"
  status      public.deliverable_status not null default 'pending',
  due_date    date,
  assigned_to uuid references public.profiles(id),
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  constraint uq_campaign_deliverables_phase unique (campaign_id, phase)  -- prevents duplicate phase ordering
);
```

### Indexes

```sql
create index idx_campaigns_org_id on public.campaigns(org_id);
create index idx_campaigns_brand_id on public.campaigns(brand_id);
create index idx_campaigns_status on public.campaigns(status) where status in ('planning', 'active');
create index idx_campaign_deliverables_campaign on public.campaign_deliverables(campaign_id, phase);
```

### Triggers

```sql
create trigger set_campaigns_updated_at
  before update on public.campaigns
  for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();

create trigger set_campaign_deliverables_updated_at
  before update on public.campaign_deliverables
  for each row execute function public.set_updated_at();

create trigger check_campaign_org_consistency
  before insert or update on public.campaigns
  for each row execute function public.check_campaign_org_consistency();
```

### RLS

```sql
alter table public.campaigns enable row level security;
alter table public.campaign_deliverables enable row level security;

-- campaigns
create policy "campaigns_select_org_member" on public.campaigns
  for select using (is_org_member(org_id));

create policy "campaigns_insert_org_member" on public.campaigns
  for insert with check (
    is_org_member(org_id)
    and (select auth.uid()) = (select user_id from public.brands where id = brand_id)
  );

create policy "campaigns_update_org_member" on public.campaigns
  for update using (is_org_member(org_id))
  with check (
    is_org_member(org_id)
    and (select auth.uid()) = (select user_id from public.brands where id = brand_id)
  );

create policy "campaigns_delete_org_member" on public.campaigns
  for delete using (
    is_org_member(org_id)
    and (select auth.uid()) = (select user_id from public.brands where id = brand_id)
  );

-- campaign_deliverables
create policy "campaign_deliverables_select_org_member" on public.campaign_deliverables
  for select using (
    is_org_member((select org_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
  );

create policy "campaign_deliverables_insert_owner" on public.campaign_deliverables
  for insert with check (
    is_org_member((select org_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
    and (select auth.uid()) = (select user_id from public.brands where id = (select brand_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
  );

create policy "campaign_deliverables_update_assigned_or_owner" on public.campaign_deliverables
  for update using (
    is_org_member((select org_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
  )
  with check (
    (select auth.uid()) = assigned_to
    or (select auth.uid()) = (select user_id from public.brands where id = (select brand_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
  );

create policy "campaign_deliverables_delete_owner" on public.campaign_deliverables
  for delete using (
    is_org_member((select org_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
    and (select auth.uid()) = (select user_id from public.brands where id = (select brand_id from public.campaigns where id = campaign_id))
  );
```

<example name="allowed_select">
-- Operator sees their org's campaigns (RLS filters by is_org_member)
select * from campaigns;
</example>

<example name="denied_cross_org">
-- Cross-org SELECT via anon/authenticated client returns 0 rows
-- (RLS enforces is_org_member on all operations)
-- NOT testable via supabase db query --linked (runs as service role, bypasses RLS)
-- Test via a supabase-js client authenticated as a non-member user
</example>

### FK repair: `crm_deals.campaign_id`

```sql
alter table crm_deals add constraint fk_crm_deals_campaign
  foreign key (campaign_id) references public.campaigns(id) on delete set null;
```

All SQL above is one atomic migration file.

---

## Mermaid diagrams

### ERD

```mermaid
erDiagram
    organizations ||--o{ campaigns : has
    brands ||--o{ campaigns : has
    campaigns ||--o{ campaign_deliverables : contains
    campaigns ||--o{ crm_deals : references
    campaign_deliverables }o--|| profiles : assigned

    campaigns {
        uuid id PK
        uuid org_id FK
        uuid brand_id FK
        text name
        campaign_status status
        campaign_objective_type objective
        date start_date
        date end_date
        text cover_url
        timestamptz created_at
        timestamptz updated_at
    }

    campaign_deliverables {
        uuid id PK
        uuid campaign_id FK
        smallint phase
        text label
        deliverable_status status
        date due_date
        uuid assigned_to FK
        timestamptz created_at
        timestamptz updated_at
    }
```

### Migration workflow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Dev
    participant CLI as Supabase CLI
    participant Remote as Supabase Remote
    participant App as Next.js app/

    Dev->>CLI: supabase migration new ipi268_campaigns_schema
    CLI->>Dev: migration file scaffolded
    Dev->>Dev: write types + tables + triggers + RLS + FK SQL
    Dev->>CLI: supabase db push --linked
    CLI->>Remote: apply migration
    Remote-->>CLI: success
    CLI->>Dev: verify via supabase db query --linked
    Dev->>CLI: npm run supabase:types
    CLI->>App: regenerate types.ts
    Dev->>CLI: npm run supabase:verify-rls
    CLI->>Remote: run RLS probe script
    Remote-->>CLI: 🟢 all probes
    Dev->>Dev: PR (migration-only, one concern)
```

---

## Acceptance criteria

* **A — New tables exist:** `select to_regclass('public.campaigns')` returns `campaigns`; same for `campaign_deliverables`
  * *proof:* `supabase db query --linked "select to_regclass('public.campaigns') is not null as exists;"` returns `true`
* **B — Types created:** `campaign_status` and `deliverable_status` enums exist
  * *proof:* `supabase db query --linked "select typname from pg_type where typname in ('campaign_status', 'deliverable_status');"` returns 2 rows
* **C — RLS policies exist:** 9 policies across 2 tables
  * *proof:* `supabase db query --linked "select tablename, policyname from pg_policies where tablename in ('campaigns', 'campaign_deliverables');"` lists all policies
* **D — Indexes present:** 4 named indexes
  * *proof:* `supabase db query --linked "select indexname from pg_indexes where tablename in ('campaigns', 'campaign_deliverables');"` lists idx_campaigns_org_id, idx_campaigns_brand_id, idx_campaigns_status, idx_campaign_deliverables_campaign
* **E — FK repaired:** `crm_deals.campaign_id` has constraint
  * *proof:* `supabase db query --linked "select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'crm_deals'::regclass and confrelid = 'campaigns'::regclass;"` returns `fk_crm_deals_campaign`
* **F — Triggers present:** 3 triggers exist
  * *proof:* `supabase db query --linked "select tgname from pg_trigger where tgname in ('set_campaigns_updated_at', 'set_campaign_deliverables_updated_at', 'check_campaign_org_consistency');"` returns 3 rows
* **G —** `verify-rls` **green:** `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls` exit 0
* **H — Types regenerated:** `cd app && npm run supabase:types` produces updated `src/types/supabase.ts` with campaign types
  * *proof:* `grep campaign_status app/src/types/supabase.ts` returns match

---

## Technical notes

**Do NOT:**

* Mix this migration with React code in the same PR — migration-only PR
* Skip RLS on either table — `is_org_member` on every operation
* Cover image upload in this PR — `cover_url` column exists but must only be written via RPC ([IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery))
* Create a non-org-scoped enum — campaign and deliverable statuses are org-scoped
* Add `campaign_id` FK as `on delete cascade` on `crm_deals` — use `on delete set null` (deals survive campaign deletion)
* Run `supabase db query --linked` (service role) to test RLS — it bypasses row-level security

**Out of scope:**

* Campaign UI (SCR-07 / [IPI-249](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-249/design-058-campaign-management-react-parity-workspace)) — separate React PR
* Campaign performance analytics (SCR-17 / [IPI-297](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-297/design-091-campaign-performance-react-port-drill-down)) — needs BE-D2
* Campaign cover image upload — Cloudinary pipeline ([IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery))
* Creative Director agent route wiring — after UI ships
* Add/remove milestones or template phases — deliverable phases are flexible by ordinal

---

## Completion steps

- [ ] **A — Write migration:** Create `supabase/migrations/20260707100000_ipi268_campaigns_schema.sql` with types → functions → tables → indexes → triggers → RLS → FK repair
  * *proof:* file exists and `supabase migration list --linked` shows new migration
- [ ] **B — Apply to remote:** `supabase db push --linked`
  * *proof:* AC A-G all pass
- [ ] **C — Regenerate types:** `cd app && npm run supabase:types`
  * *proof:* grep `campaign_status` `app/src/types/supabase.ts` returns match
- [ ] **D — Verify RLS:** `infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls`
  * *proof:* exit 0, all probes 🟢
- [ ] **E — Open migration-only PR** with title `feat(ipi-268): add campaigns + campaign_deliverables schema`
  * *proof:* PR checklist includes verify steps A–H

---

## Verification

```bash
# Table exists
supabase db query --linked "select to_regclass('public.campaigns') is not null as campaigns_exists, to_regclass('public.campaign_deliverables') is not null as deliverables_exists;"

# Types exist
supabase db query --linked "select typname from pg_type where typname in ('campaign_status', 'deliverable_status');"

# RLS policies exist
supabase db query --linked "select tablename, policyname from pg_policies where tablename in ('campaigns', 'campaign_deliverables');"

# Indexes
supabase db query --linked "select indexname from pg_indexes where tablename in ('campaigns', 'campaign_deliverables');"

# FK repaired
supabase db query --linked "select conname from pg_constraint where conrelid = 'crm_deals'::regclass and confrelid = 'campaigns'::regclass;"

# Triggers exist
supabase db query --linked "select tgname from pg_trigger where tgname in ('set_campaigns_updated_at', 'set_campaign_deliverables_updated_at', 'check_campaign_org_consistency');"

# Full RLS suite
infisical run -- npm run supabase:verify-rls
cd app && npm run supabase:types && grep campaign_status app/src/types/supabase.ts
```

Title: DESIGN-074 · Cloudinary Media Pipeline — Upload to Delivery
Description:
'> **SDK relationship note (2026-07-03):** [IPI-350](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-350) (+ sub-tasks [IPI-352](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-352) Node SDK, [IPI-353](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-353) next-cloudinary) is a **separate, prerequisite concern** — it swaps hand-rolled URL/signature code for the official SDKs, nothing more. It does **not** replace any of 074a–f here: no signed-upload route, no `cloudinary_assets` schema/RLS, no webhook, no DNA trigger, no transform presets, and no bulk-tag API exist without this epic. Sequencing: IPI-349 (config fix) unblocks both tracks → IPI-352/353 install the SDKs → 074a should then be built directly on `cloudinary.uploader.upload()` / `cloudinary.utils.api_sign_request()` rather than installing the SDK itself as this doc's "Existing on disk" section originally implied.

# [IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery) · DESIGN-074 · Cloudinary Media Pipeline (074a–f)

**Repo SSOT:** `docs/linear/issues/IPI-257-cloudinary-pipeline.md`

## Progress (2026-07-02)

| Sub | Status | PR |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **074b** cloudinary_assets columns + brand RLS | ✅ Done | [#154](<https://github.com/amo-tech-ai/lumina-studio/pull/154>) |
| **074a** Signed upload API | ⬜ Todo | — |
| **074c** Post-upload link + webhook | ⬜ Todo | — |
| **074d** DNA audit trigger | ⬜ Todo | — |
| **074e** Named transform presets | ⬜ Todo | — |
| **074f** Bulk tag/replace | ⬜ Todo | — |

> Epic reopened — 074b merged; **074a–f remain** before [IPI-248](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-248/design-057-asset-library-react-parity-workspace) upload UX.

## 1. Purpose

End-to-end Cloudinary pipeline: signed upload → cloudinary_assets row → DNA score → named transforms → channel-ready delivery.

## 2. User story

> As a **creative director**, I upload assets, see real thumbnails + DNA scores within seconds, with channel crops generated from one master.

## 3. Business value

Unblocks Assets ([IPI-248](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-248/design-057-asset-library-react-parity-workspace)) · Channel Preview transforms · analytics asset KPIs.

## 4. Scope

**In:** 074a–f split PRs · server-only SDK · webhook · audit-asset-dna · presets
**Out:** Assets grid UI ([IPI-248](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-248/design-057-asset-library-react-parity-workspace)) · Channel Preview UI ([IPI-269](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-269/design-060-channel-preview-react-parity-dv2-refresh))

## 5. Features

See repo spec: upload-sign · webhook verify · DNA trigger · presets (brand-cover, asset-tile, channel-ig/tiktok)

## 6. Frontend wiring (consumers)

Title: DESIGN-050 · Command Center — React Parity Workspace
Description:
# IPI-17 · DESIGN-050 — Command Center React Parity Workspace

**Linear:** [IPI-17](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-17)  
**Parent:** [IPI-254](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-254) · DESIGN V2  
**Priority:** Urgent · P0 #1 in [`tasks/plan/todo.md`](<../../tasks/plan/todo.md>)  
**Wireframe:** [`tasks/wireframes-ipix/IPI-17-DESIGN-050-command-center.wire`](<../../tasks/wireframes-ipix/IPI-17-DESIGN-050-command-center.wire>)  
**Status:** Done · merged [#168 ](<https://github.com/amo-tech-ai/lumina-studio/pull/168>)`9eba76f` · 2026-07-01

---

## Plain English

After this ships, operators land on `/app` and see a **portfolio-first dashboard** — live system status, brand hero, recent work, pending approvals, and one-click next actions — inside the **3-panel OperatorShell**, not a link grid.

**Blocked by:** nothing hard · **Unblocks:** operator first impression · KPI routing · AI context on every session

---

## The problem this solves

* Today `/app` is a **card router** (~15% DC) with placeholder status strings — not the main dashboard from [`Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html`](<../../Universal%20design%20prompt/Command%20Center.v2.image-first.dc.html>).
* No **OperatorShell** — page is standalone; IntelligencePanel / NavSidebar not wired on home.
* No **Supabase KPI reads** — counts are fake; approvals strip missing.
* Operators don't see **highest-priority action** on login (approvals, deliverables, shoot plan).

**Fix:** DC parity upgrade on existing route — preserve IPI-11 zero-brand → onboarding redirect.

---

## User story

> As an **operator**, when I open FashionOS after login,  
> I see my portfolio hero, recent work, and pending approvals with clear next actions,  
> so I can decide what to do in one glance without hunting workspace cards.

---

## Design reference

| Source | Path |
| -- | -- |
| **DC prototype (SSOT visual)** | [`Universal design prompt/Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html`](<../../Universal%20design%20prompt/Command%20Center.v2.image-first.dc.html>) |
| Handoff screen map | [`tasks/design-docs/handoff/02-screen-map.md`](<../../tasks/design-docs/handoff/02-screen-map.md>) §1 |
| Checklist | [`tasks/design-docs/handoff/11-screen-checklists.md`](<../../tasks/design-docs/handoff/11-screen-checklists.md>) · Command Center |
| State map | [`tasks/design-docs/handoff/08-state-map.md`](<../../tasks/design-docs/handoff/08-state-map.md>) |
| Agent map | [`tasks/design-docs/handoff/06-ai-workflows.md`](<../../tasks/design-docs/handoff/06-ai-workflows.md>) · `production-planner` |
| Implementation map | [`tasks/design-docs/handoff/09-react-implementation-map.md`](<../../tasks/design-docs/handoff/09-react-implementation-map.md>) |

---

## Current production state
