| Route `/app` | ✅ `CommandCenter` component | Not SectionPlaceholder |
| Layout | 🔴 standalone page | No OperatorShell 3-panel |
| KPI data | 🔴 placeholder strings | No Supabase reads |
| DC hero / portfolio | 🔴 | Card grid only |
| Recent work row | 🔴 | Missing |
| Realtime status strip | 🔴 | Missing |
| Approvals strip | 🔴 | Stub count/card OK · **soft dependency:** [IPI-244](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-244) for full queue |
| Agent | 🟡 | production-planner via route map ✅ |
| Zero-brand redirect | ✅ IPI-11 | Preserve |

**Code today:** `app/src/components/command-center/command-center.tsx` · `app/src/app/(operator)/app/page.tsx`

---

## Wireframe

### Desktop (OperatorShell)

```text
┌ Nav ─┬──────── Command Center workspace ──────────────────┬─ Intel ─┐
│Brand │ [● Live] Realtime status strip          [Refresh]   │ Portfolio│
│rail  │ ┌ hero MediaCard ─────────────────────────────┐    │ summary  │
│Work- │ │ cover · "You're working with {brand}"      │    │ chips    │
│spaces│ │ quick: [Deliverables][Approvals][Shoot]    │    │          │
│      │ └────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │ Copilot  │
│      │ Recent work ─────────────────────── [View all]    │ sidebar  │
│      │ ┌tile┐ ┌tile┐ ┌tile┐ ┌tile┐ → scroll            │          │
│      │ [ApprovalCard compact/large if pending]           │          │
│      │ PersistentChatDock                                │          │
└──────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────┘
```

### Mobile

Bottom tabs · Intel as sheet · chat dock above tabs · status strip full width.

**Wire DSL:** [`IPI-17-DESIGN-050-command-center.wire`](<../../tasks/wireframes-ipix/IPI-17-DESIGN-050-command-center.wire>)

---

## States

| State | What to show |
| -- | -- |
| **populated** | Hero + recent row + quick chips + optional compact approval |
| **loading** | Skeleton hero + skeleton recent row (no spinner) |
| **empty** | Welcome hero + "Set up brand" → onboarding |
| **error** | Inline error + Retry · Report |
| **approval** | Prominent ApprovalCard (HITL) + thinking indicator |
| **realtime strip** | live · reconnecting · stale · blocked (+ Refresh / Request access) |

Brand row in nav → `/app/brand/:id`

---

## Flow diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Login["/login"] --> App["/app page.tsx"]
  App --> Zero{brand count = 0?}
  Zero -->|yes| OB["/app/onboarding IPI-11"]
  Zero -->|no| Shell[OperatorShell]
  Shell --> KPI[Supabase KPI reads]
  KPI --> CC[CommandCenter workspace]
  CC --> Hero[Portfolio hero]
  CC --> Recent[Recent work row]
  CC --> Approve[ApprovalCard IPI-244 soft]
  CC --> Strip[Realtime status strip]
  Shell --> Panel[IntelligencePanel IPI-243]
  Shell --> Agent[production-planner IPI-259 soft]
  Hero --> BD["/app/brand/:id"]
  Recent --> SD["/app/shoots/:id"]
  Approve --> HITL[approve/reject action]
```

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Op as Operator
  participant Page as /app page
  participant SB as Supabase
  participant CC as CommandCenter
  participant Panel as IntelligencePanel
  participant Agent as production-planner

  Op->>Page: GET /app
  Page->>SB: brands count + latest brand
  Page->>SB: recent shoots + pending drafts
  Page->>CC: render with data
  CC->>Panel: portfolio context
  Op->>Agent: chip "Plan a shoot"
  Agent-->>Op: navigate + draft plan
```

---

## Scope

* Wrap `/app` in **OperatorShell** (DESIGN-030)
* Replace card grid with DC workspace sections per prototype
* **Supabase reads:** brand count · latest brand hero · recent shoots · `pending_approval` draft count
* **Realtime status strip** (UI states; live wiring soft)
* **ApprovalCard** on dashboard when pending (compact → large)
* **production-planner** context + contextual chips (not generic greeting)
* **5 states** + mobile shell pattern
* Preserve **IPI-11** redirect

## Out of scope

* Campaigns / Matching / Assets full screens
* EvidenceBlock modal wiring → IPI-172
* Playwright bootstrap → IPI-258 (manual smoke OK for MVP)
* New migrations (read existing tables only)

Title: SCR-22 · Booking Detail
Description:
## SCR-22 — Booking Detail

**Priority:** P2 · **Blocked by:** [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard) (SCR-21 Booking Wizard) · [IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only) (BE-B0b booking agent)

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-22-booking-detail.md`

**Route:** `/app/bookings/[id]` (greenfield — does not exist)

**Design source:** `Pages/Shoot Detail.v2.image-first.dc.html` (authoring shortcut — booking reuse of shoot detail layout patterns)

**Current state:** 🔴 No route exists. No BookingDetail component exists.

---

## Overview

Booking status detail with FSM actions, history timeline, participant info, and IntelligencePanel context. Uses DetailShell layout pattern (RF-A1b deferred — build standalone first).

## Purpose

Operator views booking status (requested→quoted→confirmed), takes transition actions, and sees communication history.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Booking wizard completed] -->|redirect| B[Booking detail /app/bookings/[id]]
  B --> C[Status stepper: requested → quoted → confirmed]
  B --> D[FSM action buttons: confirm, cancel, reschedule]
  B --> E[History timeline: AI drafts, messages, status changes]
  B --> F[Participants: talent, operator, crew]
  B --> G[IntelligencePanel: booking summary + NBA]
  B --> H[Chat dock: booking agent context]
```

## Component reuse

| Component | Usage | Reuse from |
| -- | -- | -- |
| StatusChip | Booking status badge | RF-01 |
| EmptyState | No activity | RF-A7b |
| ErrorState | Error | RF-A7b |
| IntelligencePanel | Booking context | Existing shell |
| DetailShell | Tab + hero layout | RF-A1b (future — build standalone) |
| ApprovalCard | HITL for status transitions | Existing |
| N/A | NO copy of shoot detail | Pattern reuse only, distinct layout |

## Backend data

| Data | Source | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Booking | `get_booking` RPC | 🟢 live |
| Status history | `booking_status_history` | 🟢 live |
| Participants | booking record fields | 🟢 live |
| Messages | `booking_messages` or activity log | 🟢 verify |
| Transition | `transition_booking` RPC | 🟢 live |
| Confirmation | `confirm_booking` RPC | 🟢 live |
| Real-time updates | BE-RT1 (Supabase Realtime) | 🔴 [IPI-401](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-401/be-rt1-realtime-notifications-bookings) not started |
| Booking agent | Mastra `booking` agent | 🔴 [IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only) |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | Route `/app/bookings/[id]` exists | browser navigates |
| 2 | Booking loads by id — header shows status, talent name, date, value | browser renders |
| 3 | Status stepper shows current stage: requested→quoted→confirmed | visual match |
| 4 | FSM action buttons: transition_booking actions per status | click → RPC |
| 5 | History timeline: booking created, AI draft, status changes | data from DB |
| 6 | Participant info: talent profile link, operator info | navigable links |
| 7 | AI summary: booking agent context in IntelligencePanel | panel shows summary |
| 8 | Empty: no activity → EmptyState | truncate data |
| 9 | Loading: skeleton | visible during fetch |
| 10 | Error: ErrorState with retry | mock failure |
| 11 | Not found: invalid id → 404 | notFound() |
| 12 | tokens.css only — no legacy hex | grep check |

## Mobile & a11y

| # | Requirement |
| -- | -- |
| 1 | Status stepper readable at 390px (horizontal scroll if needed) |
| 2 | FSM action buttons >=44px |
| 3 | Timeline keyboard-navigable |
| 4 | Touch targets on all action buttons |

## AI boundaries

| Area | Status |
| -- | -- |
| Booking agent | 🔴 [IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only) — not started, no context possible |
| IntelligencePanel | 🟡 generic until agent exists |
| Chat dock | 🟡 placeholder |
| AI writes | 🔴 none — wizard handles drafting, this is read-only |
| Status transitions | 🟢 direct RPC calls (no AI in the loop for non-terminal) |

## Test plan

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && CI=true npm run build
```

## Out of scope

* Real-time updates (BE-RT1 — follow-up)
* Multi-party confirmation flow
* Payment/invoicing
* Calendar sync
* Mobile shell (<1024px)

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
| -- | -- |
| 🔴 BLOCKER: [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard) (Booking Wizard) must exist first | Detail has nothing to show without a booking |
| 🔴 BLOCKER: Booking agent ([IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only)) not started | Detail works without agent (RPCs live) but IntelligencePanel context is generic |
| DetailShell not extracted (RF-A1b) | Build standalone; extraction is follow-up |

Title: SCR-21 · Booking Wizard
Description:
## SCR-21 — Booking Wizard

**Priority:** P2 · **Blocked by:** [IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only) (BE-B0b Booking Mastra agent) · **Dependencies:** SCR-20 (Talent Profile) · [IPI-385](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-385/rf-01-extract-statuschip-crm-status-tokens)/386/387 (shared atoms)

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-21-booking-wizard.md`

**Route:** `/app/matching/talent/[id]/book` (greenfield — does not exist)

**Design source:** `Pages/Shoot Wizard.v2.image-first.dc.html` (authoring shortcut — different route context, same wizar layout)

**Current state:** 🔴 No route exists. No BookingWizard component exists. No booking agent exists (BE-B0b, [IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only), 🔴 Not Started).

---

## Overview

Multi-step booking wizard: select talent (from SCR-09/20), set rate/dates/message, AI-drafts a booking quote via the booking Mastra agent, operator reviews and confirms via HITL. Booking agent **never writes directly to DB** — operator approves via ApprovalCard before `transition_booking` RPC.

## Purpose

Operator creates a booking request for a talent profile in a guided wizard with AI assistance.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Talent profile SCR-20] -->|"Request booking" CTA| B[Booking wizard /app/matching/talent/[id]/book]
  B --> C[Step 1: Select shoot context]
  C --> D[Step 2: Set rate + dates + message]
  D --> E[Step 3: AI drafts quote via booking agent]
  E --> F[HITL review: ApprovalCard]
  F -->|Approve| G[create_booking_request RPC]
  F -->|Edit| D
  F -->|Reject| H[Cancelled state]
  G --> I[Booking detail /app/bookings/[id]]
```

| WizardShell | Full-screen step container | RF-A1 (future — currently embedded in shoots/new) |

| ApprovalCard | HITL draft review | Existing component |
| StatusChip | Booking status | RF-01 |
| Chat dock | Booking agent context | Existing, agentId=`booking` |
| N/A | NO custom wizard logic | Uses WizardShell pattern |
| N/A | NO new RPC | Uses existing `create_booking_request` |

## Backend data

| -- | -- | -- |
| Talent profile | `search_talent` | 🟢 live |
| Booking create | `create_booking_request` RPC | 🟢 live |
| Booking agent | Mastra `booking` agent (draft-only) | 🔴 [IPI-397](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-397/be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only) — not started |

| Transition | `transition_booking` RPC | 🟢 live |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |

| 1 | Route `/app/matching/talent/[id]/book` exists | browser navigates |
| 2 | Multi-step wizard: select shoot, set rate/dates/message, review | steps navigable |

* Agent **never writes directly to DB** — no `create_booking_request` or `transition_booking` in tools

Title: BE-B0b · Booking Agent Verification & Finalization
Description:
## BE-B0b — Booking Agent Verification & Finalization

**Track:** Backend · **Priority:** P2 (agent shipped, UI unblocks via [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)/411) · **Complexity:** S
**SSOT:** `tasks/backend/BE-B0b-booking-mastra-agent.md`
**Branch (agent code, shipped):** `ai/ipi-397-be-b0b-booking-mastra-agent-draft-only`
**Unblocks:** SCR-21 ([IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)), SCR-22 ([IPI-411](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-411/scr-22-booking-detail))

This issue verifies and finalizes the booking Mastra agent. The backend implementation (agent + tools + registry + route mapping) is **95% shipped** on `main`. What remains is verification: draft-only guarantees, HITL gate, CopilotKit resolution, and snapshot tests.

---

## User story

> As an operator drafting a talent booking, I want the AI to draft messages and rate quotes based on the shoot brief and talent profile — without committing anything to the database — so I can review and approve before the booking is created.

---

## Data flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant O as Operator
    participant CK as CopilotKit Dock
    participant BA as booking Agent
    participant BT as booking-tools.ts
    participant DB as Supabase RPCs

    O->>CK: "Draft booking for Maria (Zara)"
    CK->>BA: getLocalAgents() → booking
    BA->>BT: checkTalentAvailability(talentId, dates)
    BT->>DB: search_talent + get_booking
    DB-->>BT: availability + rate card
    BT-->>BA: slots data
    BA->>BT: draftBookingQuote(talentId, brief, dates)
    BT-->>BA: rate quote + message draft (read-only, no RPC)
    BA-->>CK: HITL approval card
    CK->>O: Review draft quote
    Note over O,DB: Operator approves → createBookingDraft with operatorConfirmed: true
    O->>CK: Approve
    CK->>BA: createBookingDraft(operatorConfirmed: true)
    BA->>BT: createBookingDraft with operatorConfirmed: true
    BT->>DB: create_booking_request
```

---

## Current state (all verified on `main`)

### Agent infra — shipped

| Component | Path | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent definition | `app/src/mastra/agents/booking-agent.ts` | ✅ Exists on disk |
| Agent export | `app/src/mastra/agents/index.ts:58` | ✅ Exported |
| Registry | `app/src/mastra/index.ts:23` — `booking: bookingAgent` | ✅ Registered |
| Tools | `app/src/mastra/tools/booking-tools.ts` | ✅ 3 tools (checkTalentAvailability, draftBookingQuote, createBookingDraft) |
| Tool tests | `app/src/mastra/tools/booking-tools.test.ts` | ✅ 10 tests passing |
| Route mapping | `app/src/lib/route-agent-map.ts:16-18` | ✅ Maps /app/bookings, /app/model, /app/roster → "booking" |
| CopilotKit runtime | `api/copilotkit/[[...slug]]/route.ts` | ✅ getLocalAgents includes booking |

### Booking RPCs — shipped (do not recreate)

All verified on Supabase remote:

* `talent.bookings` + `version` optimistic lock
* `create_booking_request`, `transition_booking`, `confirm_booking`, `get_booking`, `list_bookings`
* `search_talent`, `toggle_shortlist_item`

---

## Draft-only guarantees (already shipped)

The agent is **hard-gated** against accidental writes:

| Guard | Location | What it does |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent instructions line 28 | `booking-agent.ts:28` | "You NEVER confirm or approve a booking — no confirm_booking tool exists" |
| Agent instructions line 29 | `booking-agent.ts:29` | "Never call createBookingDraft without explicit operator approval" |
| Tool schema guard | `booking-tools.ts:155-157` | `operatorConfirmed` must be `true` — documented in schema |
| Tool runtime guard | `booking-tools.ts:175-178` | Throws `Error` if `operatorConfirmed` is false — blocks write before RPC call |
| Test coverage | `booking-tools.test.ts:176-189` | Test asserts throw + no RPC call when `operatorConfirmed: false` |
| No confirm tool | `booking-tools.ts:3` | "Never expose confirm_booking — confirmation is human-only via POST .../approve" |
| Read-only tool | `booking-tools.ts:126` | `draftBookingQuote` described as "Read-only — does not write to the database" |
| Read-only impl | `booking-tools.ts:139` | `execute: async (input) => buildQuoteDraft(input)` — pure function, no DB call |

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Agent instructions explicitly enforce draft-only behavior (verified: ✅ already shipped, lines 28-31)
- [ ] B. Agent never invokes persistence RPCs without `operatorConfirmed: true` (verified: ✅ blocked at tool level, lines 175-178)
- [ ] C. HITL approval required before booking creation (verified: ✅ agent instructions + tool guard)
- [ ] D. Booking quote generation works: `draftBookingQuote` returns local draft, zero RPC calls (verified: ✅ pure function, test asserts no RPC)
- [ ] E. Availability lookup works: `checkTalentAvailability` calls RPC and returns candidate result (verified: ✅ tool + test)
- [ ] F. CopilotKit resolves `agentId="booking"` correctly from route-agent-map → Mastra registry → runtime (verify with `grep -rn '"booking"' app/src/mastra/`)
- [ ] G. Snapshot test added for agent instructions (`booking-agent.snapshot.test.ts`) to prevent prompt drift
- [ ] H. Integration test added: user → booking agent → availability → quote → draft returned → NO booking RPC
- [ ] I. `cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test` green
- [ ] J. Booking Wizard (SCR-21, [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)) and Booking Detail (SCR-22, [IPI-411](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-411/scr-22-booking-detail)) remain explicitly out of scope

---

## Out of scope

* Booking Wizard UI (SCR-21, [IPI-410](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-410/scr-21-booking-wizard)) — separate frontend task
* Booking Detail UI (SCR-22, [IPI-411](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-411/scr-22-booking-detail)) — separate frontend task
* Calendar sync or external booking integrations
* `/app/bookings`, `/app/model`, `/app/roster` page creation (route mapping already shipped)

---

## Suggested test additions

### Snapshot test: `booking-agent.snapshot.test.ts`

```typescript
// Verify agent instructions contain draft-only wording
// When instructions change, snapshot update requires explicit review
import { bookingAgent } from "./booking-agent";

it("instructions enforce draft-only behavior", () => {
  expect(bookingAgent.instructions).toContain("NEVER confirm or approve");
  expect(bookingAgent.instructions).toContain("operatorConfirmed");
  expect(bookingAgent.instructions).not.toContain("confirm_booking");
});
```

### Integration test

```typescript
// Verify end-to-end: user asks for draft → no RPC write
it("draft-only flow does not call persistence RPCs", async () => {
  const result = await bookingAgent.execute("Draft booking for Maria for Aug 1-3");
  // Assert: availability was checked
  // Assert: quote was drafted locally
  // Assert: NO create_booking_request was called (no operatorConfirmed)
});
```

### Regression test

```typescript
it('agent responds with draft, not confirmation, to "Book Maria"', async () => {
  const result = await bookingAgent.execute("Book Maria for Aug 1-3");
  expect(result.text).not.toMatch(/Booking created|Confirmed|Booked/i);
  expect(result.text).toMatch(/draft|prepared|ready|review/i);
});
```

---

## Verification

```bash
# Agent exists and is registered
grep -rn '"booking"' app/src/mastra/
# Should show: index.ts:23, agents/index.ts:58, route-agent-map.ts:16-18

# Existing tests pass
cd app && npx vitest run src/mastra/tools/booking-tools.test.ts
cd app && npx vitest run src/mastra/agents/index.test.ts

# Route agent map correct
grep -A1 "booking" app/src/lib/route-agent-map.ts

# Full app gate
cd app && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test
```

## Skills

`mastra` · `copilotkit` · `gemini` · `task-verifier` · `ipix-task-lifecycle`

## Failure points (pre-mortem)

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Agent accidentally writes bookings | 🔴 High | operatorConfirmed guard + instructions ban auto-commit |
| createBookingDraft tool performs persistence | 🟡 Medium | Intentional — only after operatorConfirmed: true |
| Route map drift (registry ↔ runtime ↔ agent map) | 🟡 Medium | grep verify in AC-F |
| RPC drift (agent calls wrong RPC) | 🟡 Medium | No booking RPC exists as tool without operatorConfirmed guard |
| Agent prompt drift removes draft-only wording | 🟢 Low | Snapshot test AC-G catches this |

Title: SCR-30 · CRM Pipeline — React Parity
Description:
## SCR-30 — CRM Pipeline React Parity

**Priority:** P1 · **Dependencies:** [IPI-385](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-385/rf-01-extract-statuschip-crm-status-tokens)/386/387 · [IPI-388](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-388/rf-03-crm-companies-contacts-list-screens) (CRM lists) — list screens should be functional first

**SSOT:** `Universal-design-prompt-new/tasks/screens/SCR-30-crm-pipeline.md`

**Route:** `/app/crm/pipeline`

**Design source:** `Pages/SCR-30-CRM-Pipeline.dc.html`

**Current state:** 7-line CrmScreenGate stub

---

## Overview

6-stage kanban pipeline board (Lead → Qualified → Proposal → Negotiation → Won → Lost) with horizontal scroll, deal cards, stage-category health score in IntelligencePanel. Won/Lost stages are locked behind an **approval gate** — no drag, button, or agent may bypass it.

## Purpose

Operator visualizes all deals by stage, moves deals between non-terminal stages, and views deal health without leaving the board.

## User journey

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Pipeline /app/crm/pipeline] --> B[6 horizontal columns: Lead → Lost]
  B --> C[Deal cards: company, value, risk dot, days in stage]
  C --> D[IntelligencePanel: health score + NBA on selection]
  C --> E[Click deal → Deal Detail /app/crm/pipeline/[id]]
  B --> F[Lead→Negotiation: real-time move]
  B --> G[Won/Lost: approval gate only]
```

## Component reuse verification

| Component | Usage | Reuse from |
| -- | -- | -- |
| StatusChip | Deal stage chip in cards | RF-01 |
| EmptyState | No deals in pipeline | RF-A7b |
| ErrorState | Error with retry | RF-A7b |
| IntelligencePanel | Health score per selected deal | Existing shell |
| ApprovalCard | Won/Lost gate confirmation | Existing component |
| N/A | NO kanban library | CSS horizontal scroll + columns |
| N/A | NO copy of existing deal layout | Deal card inline, detail = SCR-31 |

## Data source table

| Section | Source | Status |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Deals by stage | `crm_deals` filtered by org + stage | 🟢 live |
| Deal value | `deals.value` | 🟢 live |
| Stage | `deals.stage` (lead/qualified/proposal/negotiation/won/lost) | 🟢 live |
| Risk | `deals.risk_score` or derived from activity recency | 🟡 verify |
| Days in stage | `deals.stage_entered_at` computed diff | 🟢 live |
| Health factors | crm-assistant or derived from activity data | 🟡 verify agent |
| Pipeline total | Aggregate of deal values | 🟢 computed |
| Intel panel | crm-assistant route context | 🟢 wired |

## DC grid spec

| Property | DC value |
| -- | -- |
| Shell | `56px | minmax(0,1fr) | 320px` (fixed-3col) |
| Column width | 210px fixed |
| Column header | padding 11px 13px, colored dot, label, count, value |
| Deal card | padding 10px 11px, border-radius var(--r-md), company/value/risk/days |
| Risk chip | inline-flex, gap 4px, font 10px/600 |
| Won/Lost column | Lock icon + "Enter via approval only" gated badge |
| "At risk" filter toggle | Toggle button, active=red border+bg |
| Scroll | `overflow-x:auto` on pipeline container |
| Pipeline total | h1 £52,000 mono next to "Pipeline" title |

## Acceptance criteria

| # | Criterion | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | 6 kanban columns render (Lead → Lost) with correct colors | browser: matches DC colors |
| 2 | Deal cards show company name, value, risk dot+label, days in stage | visual match to DC |
| 3 | Pipeline total value renders in header | aggregate from deals |
| 4 | Horizontal scroll when columns overflow | window resize |
| 5 | Won/Lost columns show lock icon + approval gate | click → ApprovalCard |
| 6 | "At risk only" filter toggle narrows to at-risk deals | toggle works |
| 7 | Selecting a deal updates IntelligencePanel with health breakdown | panel shows factors |
| 8 | Clicking a deal card routes to `/app/crm/pipeline/[id]` | navigates |
| 9 | Empty state: no deals → EmptyState | truncate data |
| 10 | Loading: skeleton columns | visible during fetch |
| 11 | Error: ErrorState with retry | mock failure |
| 12 | tokens.css only — no legacy hex | grep check |
| 13 | CrmScreenGate removed | component replaced |

## Won/Lost approval gate (must match SCR-31 design)

When a user clicks Won or Lost stage button:

1. An ApprovalCard overlay appears with deal summary + reason
2. "Cancel" and "Approve" buttons
3. Approving transitions the deal stage
4. Won deals show a conversion banner ("Converted to brand")
5. This is the ONLY path to set won/lost — no drag, no button, no agent bypass

## Mobile & a11y

| # | Requirement |
| -- | -- |
| 1 | Columns wrap to stage accordion at <1024px (per CRM-HANDOFF.md) |
| 2 | Keyboard navigation between deal cards (arrow keys, Enter to open) |
| 3 | Risk indicator has text label, not color-only |
| 4 | Touch targets >=44px on deal cards and filter |
| 5 | Horizontal scroll indication (fade edges or scroll hint) |

## AI boundaries

| Area | Status |
| -- | -- |
| crm-assistant | 🟢 route context for panel |
| IntelligencePanel | 🟢 health factors + NBA per deal |
| Chat dock | 🟡 placeholder |
| AI writes | 🔴 none (moves are user-initiated, approval gate) |
| Stage transitions | 🟢 direct write for Lead→Negotiation, ApprovalCard for Won/Lost |

## Test plan

```bash
cd app && npm run typecheck && npm run lint && npm test && CI=true npm run build
```

Browser: 6 columns render · deal cards show data · won/lost approval gate works · at-risk filter works · 390px accordion works · no console errors

## Out of scope

* Drag-and-drop (keyboard/card click move is MVP pattern per CRM-HANDOFF.md)
* Deal detail page (SCR-31 — separate issue)
* Creating/editing deals
* Pipeline analytics widgets

Empty = honest state or hide the tab. ## Key correction from prior scope ```mermaid flowchart TD A["IPI-371 Phase 1
⏪ THIS ISSUE"] --> B["Overview parity (token fix)"] A --> C["Shot list DC parity"] A --> D["Deliverables DC parity"] A --> E["Assets masonry grid"] A --> F["Budget bar + lines"] A --> G["Schedule timeline"] A --> H["Team crew cards"] A --> I["Approval cards"] A --> J["Activity timeline"] K["⏭️ Phase 2 — separate issues"] --> L["IPI-210 inline edit (respin)"] K --> M["IPI-217 shot CRUD (respin)"] K --> N["IPI-216 deliverable CRUD (respin)"] K --> O["IPI-213 approval writes (respin)"] K --> P["IPI-211 archive (respin)"] K --> Q["IPI-212 clone (respin)"] K --> R["IPI-214 export (respin)"] ``` ## # IPI-336 · DESIGN-053 — Onboarding React Parity

**Parent:** [IPI-254](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-254) · DESIGN V2
**Depends on:** [IPI-46](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-46) ✅ (orchestration + profile persistence)
**Repo spec:** `docs/linear/issues/IPI-336-design-053-onboarding-react-parity.md`

---

## 1. Purpose

Sign-up funnel that collects brand basics, runs AI analysis, shows Brand DNA payoff, then opens the operator app. **Standalone layout** — no OperatorShell (handoff §11).

## 2. Design source

`Universal design prompt/Onboarding.v2.zeely.dc.html`

## 3. Route

`/app/onboarding`

## 4. User story

> As a **new brand operator**, I complete a guided 13-screen funnel, see my Brand DNA results, and land on Command Center ready to plan content — without re-entering data the app already captured.

## 5. Frontend scope

* 13-screen Zeely funnel with progress segments
* Per-screen validation (inline errors before Next)
* Screen 12 analysis progress → Screen 13 DNA payoff
* Social-proof tiles + review dock per DC
* **No OperatorShell** — full-width standalone chrome
* Mobile: stacked steps, sticky progress, 44px targets
* States: per-step valid/invalid · analysing (12) · ready (13) · error + Retry

## 6. Backend / data wiring

* Reuse [IPI-46](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-46) orchestration: `createOrgAndBrandShell` → `brand-intelligence({ brandId })` → scores
* Supabase: `brands`, `brand_scores`, `organizations` (existing RLS)
* Loading: skeleton on analysis screen · error: block redirect, show Retry
* Exit: **Open FashionOS** → `/app`

## 7. CopilotKit / Intelligence Panel

* **Agent:** `brand-intelligence` (route map)
* Inline **review dock** on analysis screens — contextual chips, not generic greeting
* No right Intelligence Panel (standalone funnel)

## 8. Wireframe

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  S1[Welcome] --> S2[Brand basics]
  S2 --> S12[Analysis progress]
  S12 --> S13[DNA payoff]
  S13 --> CC[/app Command Center]
```

## 9. Implementation steps

| Step | Work | Proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| A | Audit prod wizard vs DC — gap table | audit md |
| B | Screen components + progress rail | visual vs HTML |
| C | Wire IPI-46 orchestration on final submit | 1 brand row test |
| D | Analysis + DNA payoff screens | states 12–13 |
| E | Mobile + a11y pass | 390px screenshots |

## 10. Testing

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npm test && npx tsc --noEmit && CI=true npm run build
```

Browser: DC HTML vs `/app/onboarding` · 1280/768/390 · DevTools + Playwright · `docs/qa/screenshots/YYYY-MM-DD/onboarding/`

## 11. Production-ready definition

- [ ] lint · test · typecheck · build pass
- [ ] no console errors · design matches HTML · responsive verified

## 12. Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 13 DC screens in React flow
- [ ] Per-screen validation · DNA payoff · Open FashionOS → `/app`
- [ ] IPI-46 orchestration preserved · Zeely tokens only

## Out of scope

OperatorShell · Brand Detail IPI-271 · Firecrawl IPI-24

## The problem this solves

* Today, `/app` Command Center has correct **structure** (status strip → hero → chips → recent work) but **zero fashion imagery** — grey placeholder boxes only.
* Operators see an empty-feeling dashboard (~42% DC visual parity) despite functional KPI reads shipping in IPI-17.

**Fix:** Minimal-diff polish pass — wire Cloudinary/local images, match DC card anatomy from Component Library, ship evidence + new PR (not reopen #168).

## User story

> As an **operator** on Command Center, when I land on `/app`, I see my active brand and recent shoots with **real fashion photos**, dense card rhythm, and production next actions — matching the DC prototype — so I trust the workspace as a fashion ops cockpit, not an empty scaffold.

# IPI-294 · CC-HITL-001 — Approval preview + empty state polish

**Linear:** [IPI-294](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-294)

## Skills to run

| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | `design-md` | Read `design.md` — EmptyState + HITL preview patterns |
| 2 | `claude-design-handoff` | ApprovalCard.dc.html · EmptyState.dc.html |
| 3 | `frontend-design` | 236×4:5 preview · 200×150 empty fade |
| 4 | `shadcn` | Card chrome alignment |

| 6 | `graphify` | Optional — approvals + empty + CSS module |
| 7 | `lean` | Stub only — no IPI-244 workflow |

---

## The problem this solves

Approval state shows thinking dots + text fallback or brand-hub DNA `ApprovalCard` — not DC L256–301 large preview (236×4:5 AI result + ref thumb). Empty state uses grey block instead of DC L196 faded 200×150 editorial preview.

**Fix:** Dashboard approval stub with preview image; empty state uses EmptyState DC pattern. Full Approve/Edit/Discard → IPI-244.

---

## Scope guard

**In scope:** `CommandCenterApprovals` · `CommandCenterEmpty` · approval/empty CSS  
**Out of scope:** Full HITL workflow (IPI-244) · OperatorShell · IntelligencePanel rewrite · schema

---

## Image loading

* Approval preview: 236×4:5 lazy image from sample pool
* Empty preview: 200×150 decorative · `alt=""` if purely decorative

> As an **operator** with pending drafts, I see a visual approval card with a fashion preview image — not just a text count — so I know what needs my review.

---

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
```

## Skills to run

Title: CC-IMG-001 · Cloudinary sample-images module
Description:
# IPI-291 · CC-IMG-001 — Cloudinary sample-images module

**Linear:** [IPI-291](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-291)  
**Parent:** [IPI-290](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-290)  
**Plan:** `tasks/design-docs/implementation/command-center.md` § Cloudinary rules · Image priority  
**Visual target:** `tasks/design-docs/implementation/command.png`  
**Estimate:** 2 points

---

## Skills to run

| Order | Skill | Purpose |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 1 | `worktrees` | Branch `ipi/17-command-center-dc-polish` |
| 2 | `ipix-task-lifecycle` | Phase 3 implement · verify matrix |
| 3 | `cloudinary` | URL transforms · **Cloudinary MCP** `list-images` for 8-id pool |
| 4 | `vercel-react-best-practices` | Hoist static URL pool · cache URL builder |
| 5 | `gen-test` | `sample-images.test.ts` |
| 5 | `gen-test` | `sample-images.test.ts` |
| 6 | `lean` | No new abstractions beyond URL helper |
| 7 | `graphify` | Optional — blast radius if touching queries |

**Before Done:** `@task-verifier` probes in `verifier-probes-ipix.md` § IPI-290–295.

---

## The problem this solves

Hero and recent-work tiles have no `coverUrl` / `imageUrl` types or URL builder. DC prototype uses `ph()` map → `Universal design prompt/images/{n}-fashionos.jpeg` and Cloudinary `services/` folder on `dzqy2ixl0`.

**Fix:** Central `sample-images.ts` with curated DC-aligned public_ids + deterministic fallbacks per image priority chain (plan § Image priority rules).

---

## Scope guard

**In scope:** `lib/command-center/sample-images.ts` · type extensions · `.env.local` cloud name  
**Out of scope:** React component rewrites · OperatorShell · NavSidebar · IntelligencePanel · mobile · schema

---

## Cloudinary rules

* Cloud: `dzqy2ixl0`
* Transforms: `f_auto,q_auto,c_fill,g_auto`
* Hero: `w_208,h_208` · Recent: `w_276,h_345`
* No API secrets in client bundle

---

## Image priority (implement in helper)

Brand cover → approved asset → shoot image → Cloudinary sample → local design JPG → neutral placeholder

---

## User stories

### Story 1: Dev preview shows real photos

**As a** developer, **I want** `?skip=1` to use Cloudinary JPGs, **so that** layout QA matches DC without live brand assets.

**Acceptance:** Dev fixture URLs resolve to 200 OK images.

### Story 2: Live users get graceful fallbacks

**As an** operator, **I want** fashion imagery even when DB has no cover, **so that** the dashboard never shows grey-only tiles.

**Acceptance:** `heroFallbackForBrand(id)` returns stable URL per brand.

### Story 3: Env hygiene

**As an** engineer, **I want** `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0`, **so that** transforms don't 404.

---

## Design reference

| DC | `Command Center.v2.image-first.dc.html` L497–498 (`IMG` + `ph()`) |
| Local | `Universal design prompt/images/5-fashionos.jpeg` … `24-fashionos.jpeg` |
| Cloudinary | `5-fashionos_wc2p1c`, `9-fashionos_ddj5jx`, … (`asset_folder: services`) |

---

## Technical notes

**New file:** `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts`

* `cloudinaryImageUrl(publicId, { w, h })` — mirror `app/src/app/api/brands/[id]/assets/route.ts`
* `heroFallbackForBrand(brandId)` — hash → 8-id pool (DC keys: char, clay, denim, …)
* `recentFallbackForShoot(shootId, index)` — same pool, no adjacent duplicates
* Default cloud: `process.env.CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME ?? "dzqy2ixl0"`

**Do NOT:** Commit `.env.local` · expose API secret in client · add migration · rewrite components (IPI-292+).

**Env fix (local only):** `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0` — current value has invalid suffix `/nvdlhrodvevgwdsneplk`.

**Extend types (prep for IPI-292/293):**

* `HeroBrand.coverUrl?: string | null`
* `RecentShoot.imageUrl?: string | null`

---

## API wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Returns |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Server `page.tsx` KPI fetch | ✅ exists | Supabase session | `CommandCenterData` |
| New API route | ⚪ not needed | — | fallbacks client-safe URLs only |

No migration. Optional later join `cloudinary_assets` (IPI-271).

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **A** `sample-images.ts` exports URL builder + 8 curated public_ids aligned to DC `ph()` map
- [ ] **B** Unit test: URL shape + deterministic fallback for same brandId
- [ ] **C** `DEV_PREVIEW_COMMAND_CENTER_DATA` includes sample `coverUrl` + tile `imageUrl`s
- [ ] **D** `.env.local` documents `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0` (local fix only)
- [ ] **E** No secrets in client bundle · no new env vars beyond existing Cloudinary name

## Out of scope

* React component image wiring (IPI-292/293)
* OperatorShell · NavSidebar · IntelligencePanel
* Schema migration · new API routes

---

## Completion steps

#### A. Setup

- [ ] **A1** Worktree + branch — proof: `git branch --show-current` = `ipi/17-command-center-dc-polish`
- [ ] **A2** Fix `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dzqy2ixl0` in `.env.local` — proof: grep exact line

#### B. Implement

- [ ] **B1** `sample-images.ts` + 8 public_ids — proof: vitest green
- [ ] **B2** Extend `HeroBrand.coverUrl` · `RecentShoot.imageUrl` in types.ts
- [ ] **B3** Wire URLs into `DEV_PREVIEW_COMMAND_CENTER_DATA` (coverUrl + tile imageUrls)

#### C. Verify

- [ ] **C1** `cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/command-center/sample-images.test.ts`
- [ ] **C2** `curl -I` one hero URL → HTTP 200
- [ ] **C3** `cd app && npx tsc --noEmit`
- [ ] **C4** Linear → Done

---

## Test

```bash
cd app && npx vitest run src/lib/command-center/sample-images.test.ts
```

## User stories

* As a **developer**, I reference one semantic token set (`--color-action`, `--card-radius`, …) so every operator screen matches DC without hardcoded hex.
* As a **designer**, operator and marketing share a reconciled palette/type scale, so the brand reads consistent across surfaces.
* As a **reviewer**, a grep gate proves no new component ships raw hex.

## Token flow (DC → app)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    DC["Zeely v3 tokens.css (DC)"] --> MAP["Semantic map: --color-action, --card-radius, --space-*"]
    MAP --> APP["app/src/styles/tokens.css"]
    APP --> TW["tailwind.config.ts utilities"]
    TW --> COMP["Operator components"]
    MKT["iPix marketing tokens (Cormorant + Outfit)"] -.reconcile.-> APP
```

## Reconcile decision

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    T{Token exists in app?} -->|no| ADD[Add semantic var]
    T -->|yes, same value| KEEP[Keep]
    T -->|yes, diverges| R{Operator vs marketing?}
    R -->|operator shell| USE_DC[Adopt DC value]
    R -->|marketing route| SCOPE[Scope under marketing selector]
```

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Diff `Universal design prompt/tokens.css` vs `app/src/styles/tokens.css` → produce mapping table (DC name → app name → value) | mapping table in PR |
| **B** | Add/rename semantic tokens in `app/src/styles/tokens.css`; wire into `tailwind.config.ts` | tokens resolve in a sample component |
| **C** | Reconcile marketing font tokens (Cormorant/Outfit) — no regression on `(marketing)` routes | marketing route visual unchanged |
| **D** | `rg -n '#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,6}' app/src/components` on new operator components → replace with vars | grep returns 0 in touched files |
| **E** | Visual diff vs DC Component Library at 1440 + 375; run **task-verifier** | screenshots + verifier pass |

## Out of scope

Component redesign · new screens · marketing palette overhaul. **Token layer only.**

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build
rg -n '#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,6}' app/src/components   # expect 0 in changed files
```

## Evidence required (Done gate)

- [ ] Token mapping table · grep-clean output
- [ ] 1440 + 375 screenshots vs DC
- [ ] task-verifier report · PR link · Linear → Done

# IPI-274 · DESIGN-056 — Shoot Wizard React Parity

**Parent:** [IPI-254](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-254) · **Supersedes:** [IPI-87](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-87) (Canceled)

## Scope contract (read first)

**IPI-274 is a visual reskin of the existing 6-step shoot wizard only.** Preserve all existing behavior. Do **not** add new steps, backend logic, AI tools, API routes, schema/migrations, or the booking flow. "Done" means visual parity with the DC chrome — **not** feature expansion.

## Core rule

**Don't code from Linear text alone.** Prove disk + Supabase + browser first. **HTML design wins for layout.**

**Shell:** `OperatorPanel` already wraps `/app/shoots/new` — work is **center workspace chrome only** (same as brand PR #181). Do **not** rebuild NavSidebar or IntelligencePanel.

## Production state (2026-07-05)

| -- | -- |
| Route | ✅ `shoots/new/page.tsx` ~824 LOC (client monolith, keep client) |

| WizardStep DC chrome | ❌ inline stepper |

**May edit (restyle only):**

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/new/page.tsx` — extract inline styles → CSS module, keep all handlers; stays a client component (do **not** refactor to RSC)
* `app/src/components/shoot/shoot-wizard.module.css` — **CREATE**
* `app/src/components/shoot/hitl/*ApprovalCard.tsx` (×3) — restyle only

**Must NOT edit:** `mastra/workflows/shoot-wizard.ts` · `api/shoots/commit|suggest-brief|[shootId]/route.ts` · `shoot-wizard-context.tsx` · shell/nav/intel/dock · migrations · `types/supabase.ts`

## Acceptance criteria

* Zeely tokens only — zero `#FBF8F5` / legacy hex in changed files
* Existing behavior preserved (see regression checklist below)
* §9 parity scorecard filled 🟢 (Layout · Typography · Tokens · States · Responsive · A11y) with side-by-side screenshots

## Regression checklist (must still pass after reskin)

- [ ] Back / Continue navigation between all 6 steps

- [ ] Edit / approve draft in each approval card
- [ ] Brief autogeneration (`suggest-brief`) unchanged

- [ ] Only existing endpoints called — no new routes

## Scope

* Extract **WizardStep** · apply Zeely tokens · polish **OperatorChatDock** step context
* Preserve 6-step Mastra HITL — **no workflow rewrite**
* 10-step DC expansion → [IPI-252](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-252) · booking flow → IPI-340/341/342

- [ ] E · Toast error on API failure

---

# Implementation Prompt Pack (2026-06-30)

**Worktree:** `ipi/244-approval-queue` · `../wt-ipi-244-approval-queue`
**Skills to run:** copilotkit (v2 useInterrupt) · mastra · frontend-design · shadcn · gen-test · mermaid-diagrams
**Design file (READ FIRST):** `tasks/design-docs/design/AI-EXPLAINABILITY.md` · `Universal design prompt/components/` ApprovalCard + EvidenceBlock (IPI-246) · handoff DESIGN-040 ApprovalCard parity
**MCP:** CopilotKit MCP (HITL interrupt-flow docs) · Mastra docs server

## User stories

* As a **brand guardian**, I review AI draft changes (before/after + confidence + evidence) and approve/edit/reject inline — without leaving the panel.
* As a **brand guardian**, every AI write is reversible behind a gate — nothing persists until I approve.
* As an **operator**, the pending-count badge updates immediately so I always see the queue depth.
* As a **developer**, all writes go through the existing approve route — no direct Supabase writes from UI.

## Wireframe (ApprovalQueue in panel)

```
+-----------------------------+
| Intelligence  . Pending (3) |  <- badge
| +-------------------------+ |
| | Tone: "bold" -> "refined"| |  <- before/after diff
| | confidence 82%  [why]    | |  <- EvidenceBlock (IPI-246)
| | [Approve] [Edit] [Reject]| |  <- HITL actions
| +-------------------------+ |
| | Palette draft ...        | |
| +-------------------------+ |
+-----------------------------+
```

## Card state flow

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Pending
    Pending --> Approving: click Approve
    Approving --> Approved: route 200 (green, fade-out)
    Approving --> Pending: route error (amber revert + toast)
    Pending --> Rejecting: click Reject
    Rejecting --> Removed: route 200
    Pending --> Editing: click Edit
    Editing --> Workspace: navigate to draft
```

## HITL approve sequence

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor BG as Brand Guardian
    participant Q as ApprovalQueue
    participant API as POST /api/workflows/brand-intelligence/approve
    participant M as Mastra workflow (suspended)
    participant DB as Supabase (brand_intake_drafts)
    BG->>Q: click Approve
    Q->>Q: optimistic green
    Q->>API: { runId, approved: true } (withOperatorAuth)
    API->>M: resume HITL
    M->>DB: update status (RLS)
    API-->>Q: 200
    Q-->>BG: fade-out + badge decrement
    Note over Q: on error -> amber revert + toast
```

## Frontend <-> backend wiring

| Route | Status | Auth | Body / Returns |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| `POST /api/workflows/brand-intelligence/approve` | exists | withOperatorAuth | `{ runId, approved }` -> success/error |

State: optimistic local update + SWR mutate on the drafts key. No direct Supabase from UI.

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Build `ApprovalQueue` replacing `PendingDraftsPreview`; map drafts -> cards | renders pending list |
| **B** | Card: before/after diff + confidence % + EvidenceBlock `[why]` (IPI-246) | diff + evidence show |
| **C** | Wire Approve/Reject to approve route; Edit -> navigate to draft | route called, edit nav works |
| **D** | Optimistic green->fade on 200; amber revert + toast on error; badge updates | forced-error path reverts + toasts |
| **E** | Vitest: approve success, approve error revert, badge decrement | `npm test` green |

## Out of scope

IntelligencePanel shell (IPI-243 ✅) · EvidenceBlock component itself (IPI-246) · new agent tools. Write-path UI only.

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm test && npm run build
```

Route to test: brand route with pending drafts — approve, reject, edit-nav, forced API error.

## Evidence required (Done gate)

- [ ] Screenshots: pending / approving / success / error-revert
- [ ] Vitest output · task-verifier
- [ ] PR link · Linear → Done

Title: next-cloudinary — Consolidate Hand-Rolled URL Builders
Description:
Install `next-cloudinary` ([https://github.com/cloudinary-community/next-cloudinary](<https://github.com/cloudinary-community/next-cloudinary>)) and consolidate 4 separate hand-rolled Cloudinary URL builders into one shared helper.

**Real-world example:** today 4 different files each hand-write a Cloudinary delivery URL from memory — like writing the same mailing address from scratch every time instead of using a saved contact card. One copy already has the wrong city (`dev-panel-fixture.ts` hardcodes cloud `dza2bjwwp`, not the project's real `dzqy2ixl0`); another forgot the postal code (`assets/route.ts` is missing `q_auto,f_auto` that the other builders include). `next-cloudinary`'s `getCldImageUrl`/`CldImage` is the one saved template all four should use instead.

## The problem this solves

Four independent Cloudinary URL builders exist in `app/`, each slightly different:

1. `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts:34-43` — `cloudinaryImageUrl()`, has `q_auto,f_auto`, correct cloud name (`dzqy2ixl0` via env, fallback matches)
2. `app/src/app/api/brands/[id]/assets/route.ts:11-12` — inline template string, **missing** `q_auto,f_auto` (inconsistent optimization vs. #1)
3. `app/src/lib/intelligence/dev-panel-fixture.ts:9-18` — duplicate `cloudinaryImageUrl()`, **hardcoded to the wrong cloud** (`dza2bjwwp` instead of `dzqy2ixl0`)
4. `app/src/components/media/channel-preview-studio.tsx:12` — hardcoded literal pointing at Cloudinary's public `demo` account, not this project's own

No `next-cloudinary` package is installed today — every one of these is a hand-rolled string instead of using the SDK's URL builder.

## Tasks

- [ ] Install `next-cloudinary` into `app/package.json`
- [ ] Add one shared helper (e.g. `app/src/lib/cloudinary/url.ts`) wrapping `getCldImageUrl` that all four call sites import instead of building URLs inline
- [ ] Replace `cloudinaryImageUrl()` in `sample-images.ts:34-43` with the shared helper
- [ ] Replace the inline template string in `assets/route.ts:11-12` with the shared helper (fixes missing `q_auto,f_auto`)
- [ ] Delete the duplicate, wrong-cloud `cloudinaryImageUrl()` in `dev-panel-fixture.ts:9-18` once its callers use the shared helper
- [ ] Replace the hardcoded `demo`-account fallback in `channel-preview-studio.tsx:12` with the shared helper + this project's real cloud name

## Out of scope

`CldUploadWidget`, `CldVideoPlayer`, signed-upload flows, and Assets-panel UI — those are IPI-257's own sub-deliverables (074a/074c/074f). This ticket only consolidates *read-side* delivery-URL construction into the SDK.

## Depends on

Blocked by IPI-349 (Cloudinary Config Cleanup) — the corrupted `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` and wrong hardcoded cloud in `dev-panel-fixture.ts` must be fixed first (or fixed as part of this consolidation if IPI-349 lands the env fix but not the fixture file).

## Completion proof

- [ ] `grep -rn "res.cloudinary.com" app/src` shows only the shared helper file (no more inline builders)
- [ ] `next-cloudinary` present in `app/package.json` dependencies
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` green in `app/`
- [ ] Manual: BrandCard, Assets thumbnails, dev panel, and Channel Preview all render via the shared helper against the correct cloud (`dzqy2ixl0`)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: Cloudinary Node SDK — Replace Hand-Rolled Upload/Signature Code
Description:
Swap the Mastra agent's hand-rolled Cloudinary signature/upload code for the official `cloudinary` npm SDK.

**Real-world example:** `visual-identity.ts` computes its own upload signature by hand and does a raw `fetch()` to Cloudinary's API — like mixing your own paint from raw pigments instead of buying the can that's already the exact color. It works today, but the recipe ("fields signed in alphabetical order, excluding file and api_key") is exactly what the SDK already does, tested, for every edge case — a hand-rolled copy is one typo away from silently minting bad signatures.

## The problem this solves

* `app/src/mastra/agents/visual-identity.ts` (`uploadToCloudinary()`, ~lines 60-95) reads `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME`/`CLOUDINARY_API_KEY`/`CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET` from `process.env` directly, manually computes the upload signature, and does a raw `fetch()` to `https://api.cloudinary.com/v1_1/${cloudName}/image/upload` — duplicating logic the official SDK's `uploader.upload()` already implements and maintains.
* No `cloudinary` npm package is installed in `app/package.json` today — this hand-rolled code is the *only* thing standing in for the SDK.

## Tasks

- [ ] Install `cloudinary` (official server-side SDK) into `app/package.json`
- [ ] Add one `cloudinary.config({ cloud_name, api_key, api_secret })` call from server env, reused across server code (not re-read per call)
- [ ] Replace the manual signature computation + raw `fetch()` in `uploadToCloudinary()` (`app/src/mastra/agents/visual-identity.ts`) with `cloudinary.uploader.upload()`
- [ ] Confirm no client-side/`NEXT_PUBLIC_*` exposure is introduced (secret-guard grep clean — SDK stays server-only, per this repo's Cloudinary skill note)

## Out of scope

Signed-upload API route (IPI-257 / 074a), webhook handler (074c), and Assets-panel UI — those are IPI-257's own sub-deliverables. This ticket only swaps the Mastra agent's *internal* upload call for the SDK equivalent; it does not add new upload surfaces.

## Depends on

Blocked by IPI-349 (Cloudinary Config Cleanup) — the corrupted `CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME` must be fixed first so SDK calls resolve against the correct cloud.

## Completion proof

- [ ] `grep -rn "api.cloudinary.com" app/src/mastra` returns nothing (raw fetch removed)
- [ ] `cloudinary` present in `app/package.json` dependencies
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` green in `app/`
- [ ] Manual: trigger `visual-identity` agent upload path, confirm asset lands in Cloudinary via SDK call (not raw fetch)

Title: Intelligence Panel: Route-Aware Context Sections
Description:
# IPI-286: Intelligence Panel — Route-Aware Context Sections

**Linear:** [IPI-286](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-286)  
**Design Ref:** Route-aware intelligence (adaptive panel content)  
**Parent:** IPI-255 (DESIGN-071 deferred items)  
**Priority:** Medium (P3)

---

## Context

IPI-255 shipped DNA scores and pending approvals in the Intelligence Panel. Route-aware sections were deferred as part of the original DESIGN-071 scope.

---

## Problem

The Intelligence Panel shows the same content regardless of the user's current context. When a user is planning a shoot or viewing a campaign, the panel could surface context-specific intelligence instead of generic brand data.

---

## User Stories

### Story 1: Creative Director planning shoot

**As a** Creative Director  
**I want** shoot-specific intelligence when in shoot wizard  
**So that** I see DNA criteria and asset recommendations relevant to the current shoot

**Acceptance:** Panel shows "Shoot DNA Criteria" section on `/app/shoots/new`

### Story 2: Brand Manager on brand detail page

**As a** Brand Manager  
**I want** brand-level intelligence on brand detail page  
**So that** I see DNA scores and assets specific to this brand

**Acceptance:** Panel shows "DNA Scores" + "Assets" sections on `/app/brand/[id]`

### Story 3: Campaign Manager viewing campaign

**As a** Campaign Manager  
**I want** campaign-level intelligence on campaign page  
**So that** I see multi-brand alignment and channel recommendations

**Acceptance:** Panel shows "Campaign Performance" section on `/app/campaigns/[id]`

---

## User Journey

```mermaid
journey
    title Creative Director plans Spring Campaign shoot
    section Brand Context
      Start at /app/brand/acme: 5: Director
      See DNA scores + assets in panel: 5: Director
    section Navigate to Shoot Wizard
      Click "Plan new shoot": 5: Director
      Navigate to /app/shoots/new: 5: Director
    section Shoot Context
      Panel transitions to shoot context: 5: Director
      See "Shoot DNA Criteria": Visual 80+ required: 4: Director
      See "Asset Recommendations": 12 product shots needed: 5: Director
      See "Budget Warnings": $8.5k vs $12k avg: 4: Director
    section Take Action
      Adjust deliverables based on budget warning: 5: Director
      Submit shoot plan: 5: Director
```

---

## Proposal

Make Intelligence Panel content adaptive based on the current route:

**Brand Detail (**`/app/brand/[id]`**):**

* DNA scores (current)
* Pending approvals (current)
* AI suggestions (IPI-285)
* Recent assets (IPI-284)

**Shoot Planning (**`/app/shoots/new`**,** `/app/shoots/[id]`**):**

* Shoot-specific DNA criteria
* Asset recommendations for deliverables
* Budget vs. brand standards warnings
* Similar shoot references

**Campaign View (**`/app/campaigns/[id]`**):**

* Campaign performance insights
* Multi-brand alignment scores
* Channel-specific recommendations
* Asset reuse opportunities

---

## Route Detection Logic

### ⚠️ Merge with Existing `resolveRouteBriefing`

**Existing file:** `app/src/components/intelligence-panel/route-briefing.ts`

```typescript
export function resolveRouteBriefing(pathname: string): RouteBriefing {
  // Already maps pathname → { section, headline, nextActions }
}
```

**Conflict:** Creating a separate `resolvePanelSections` will duplicate route detection logic.

**Resolution:** Extend `resolveRouteBriefing` to return section config:

```typescript
// app/src/components/intelligence-panel/route-briefing.ts (MODIFIED)
export type RouteBriefing = {
  section: string;
  headline: string;
  nextActions: string[];
  panelSections?: SectionConfig[];  // ← ADD THIS
};

export function resolveRouteBriefing(pathname: string): RouteBriefing {
  if (pathname.startsWith('/app/brand/')) {
    return {
      section: 'brand-detail',
      headline: 'Brand Intelligence',
      nextActions: ['View assets', 'Plan shoot'],
      panelSections: [  // ← ADD THIS
        { type: 'dna-scores', priority: 1 },
        { type: 'suggestions', priority: 2 },
        { type: 'assets', priority: 3 },
        { type: 'approvals', priority: 4 },
      ],
    };
  }
  // ... other routes
}
  if (pathname.startsWith('/app/brand/')) {
    return [
      { type: 'dna-scores', priority: 1 },
      { type: 'suggestions', priority: 2 },
      { type: 'assets', priority: 3 },
      { type: 'approvals', priority: 4 },
    ];
  }
  
  if (pathname.match(/\/app\/shoots\/(new|\d+)/)) {
    return [
      { type: 'shoot-dna', priority: 1 },
      { type: 'asset-recommendations', priority: 2 },
      { type: 'budget-warnings', priority: 3 },
    ];
  }
  
  if (pathname.startsWith('/app/campaigns/')) {
    return [
      { type: 'campaign-performance', priority: 1 },
      { type: 'multi-brand-alignment', priority: 2 },
      { type: 'channel-recommendations', priority: 3 },
    ];
  }
  
  return []; // Default: no sections
}
```

Title: DATA-AUDIT-001 · Investigate public.shoots vs shoot.shoots duplication (no orphan-drop bug — verified)
Description:
## DATA-AUDIT-001 — `public.shoots` vs `shoot.shoots` duplication (investigation only)

> **⚠️ Rescoped 2026-07-06 after verification (task-verifier + ipix-supabase).** The original "2 orphan shoots dropped by the view" premise was a **measurement error** — a cross-table miscount. There is **no orphan-drop bug**. This is now a read-only *investigation* of a schema duplication, **not** a data fix. **No data mutation in this issue.**

**Role:** iPix engineer (backend/data). Investigation-first — read-only until findings are reviewed.

**Plain English:** The DB has two `shoots` tables — `public.shoots` (12 rows) and `shoot.shoots` (10 rows). The Shoots List reads `shoot.shoots` (via `shoot_portfolio_view`) and shows all 10 correctly. Understand why both exist and whether `public.shoots` is stale/legacy.

**Blocked by:** none · **Skills:** `@ipix-supabase` · `@task-verifier`

---

## Verified facts (Supabase MCP, 2026-07-06)

* `shoot.shoots` = **10 rows** · `brand_id is null` = **0** · orphans (`LEFT JOIN brands` where `b.id is null`) = **0**.
* FK `shoots_brand_id_fkey → brands ON DELETE CASCADE` exists → an orphan `brand_id` in `shoot.shoots` is **impossible**.
* `information_schema.tables` shows a `shoots` table in **both** `public` and `shoot` schemas.
* The earlier "12 vs 10 = 2 orphans" compared `public.shoots` **(12)** to `shoot.shoots` **(10)** — different tables, not a drop.
* `shoot_portfolio_view` reads `shoot.shoots` and returns all 10 to the owner. **No user-facing data loss.**

---

## The actual question

* Why do `public.shoots` **and** `shoot.shoots` both exist? Which is canonical?
* Is `public.shoots` a legacy / pre-migration table that should be deprecated, or is it still written to?
* Does anything in `app/` still read `public.shoots`?

**Do NOT** mutate or drop either table in this issue — investigation only. Any cleanup is a separate, reviewed migration.

---

### Completion steps (investigation only)

#### A. Inventory (read-only)

- [ ] **A1** Diff `public.shoots` vs `shoot.shoots` (columns, row counts, latest `updated_at`) — proof: SQL result
- [ ] **A2** grep `app/` + `supabase/` for `public.shoots` / `.from("shoots")` readers — proof: grep output
- [ ] **A3** Identify the migration that created `shoot.shoots` + `shoot_portfolio_view` — proof: migration filename

#### B. Decide (write findings — do not execute)

- [ ] **B1** Recommend keep-both / deprecate `public.shoots` / consolidate — proof: 1-paragraph decision in an issue comment

#### C. Optional follow-up

- [ ] **C1** If cleanup is chosen, spin a **separate** reviewed migration issue (not this one)

---

## Out of scope

* Any `DROP` / data move (own migration issue after B1).
* Cover images → [IPI-426](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-426/shoot-cover-001-wire-real-cover-url-into-shoots-list-grid).

---

## Evidence (2026-07-06)

`select count(*) from shoot.shoots` = 10; `... where brand_id is null` = 0; orphans = 0. `pg_constraint`: `shoots_brand_id_fkey -> brands del=c`. `information_schema.tables where table_name='shoots'` → schemas `public, shoot`.

* `shoot_portfolio_view` now exposes `cover_url` (derived from `mood_board_urls[1]`) — verified live 2026-07-06 — but the app **ignores it** and renders placeholder art.
* `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts:62` literally says `TODO: replace with real shoot cover when schema adds cover_url` — that schema condition is now **true**.
* Result: every shoot card shows a generic sample photo, not the operator's actual moodboard cover → the "image-first" design intent is faked.

**Fix:** select `cover_url` from the view and render it; keep the `sample-images` helper only as the decorative fallback when `cover_url` is null.

---

## User story

> As an **Operator**, when I open Shoots List,
> I see each shoot's real moodboard cover,
> so the grid reflects my actual work instead of stock placeholders.

---

## Wireframe

```
┌───────────────────────────────┐
│  Shoots · /app/shoots         │
├───────────────────────────────┤
│  ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐      │
│  │[cover_ur│ │[cover_ur│      │  ← real moodboard[0], not sample
│  │ l img]  │ │ l img]  │      │
│  │ Name    │ │ Name    │      │
│  │ status  │ │ status  │      │
│  └─────────┘ └─────────┘      │
└───────────────────────────────┘
```

**States this UI must handle:**

| State | What to show |
| -- | -- |
| Cover present (`cover_url` not null) | render `cover_url` via `next/image` |
| Cover null (no moodboard) | decorative `shootListCoverForShoot()` fallback — clearly a placeholder, never presented as a real asset |
| Loading | existing skeleton (unchanged) |
| Error | existing error banner (unchanged) |

---

## Acceptance criteria

* **A — real cover:** cards with a moodboard render `cover_url`, not a sample image.
* **B — graceful fallback:** `cover_url = null` cards show the decorative fallback, not a broken image or a fake "uploaded asset".
* **C — no query break:** the view select uses `cover_url`; **zero** references to `cover_image` (that column does not exist → 42703).
* **D — states:** loading/error/empty unchanged from [IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first).
* **E — regression guard:** filter/search/sort + the 5 existing states still work.

---

## Technical notes

**Files to touch:**

* `app/src/app/(operator)/app/shoots/page.tsx` — add `cover_url` to the `shoot_portfolio_view` select; pass to card.
* `app/src/components/shoot/ShootCard.tsx` — use `cover_url` when present, else fallback.
* `app/src/lib/command-center/sample-images.ts` — demote `shootListCoverForShoot()` to fallback-only; drop the TODO.

**Do NOT:** select `cover_image` (column doesn't exist) · present the sample fallback as a real uploaded asset · change grid layout/tokens ([IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first) scope, already Done).

**Seeded/known data:** view column is `cover_url` (text). 8 of 10 owned shoots have it today; 2 are null → exercise the fallback path.

---

## Out of scope

* Uploading/replacing shoot covers (Assets/Cloudinary upload = [IPI-248](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-248/design-057-asset-library-react-parity-workspace) / [IPI-257](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-257/design-074-cloudinary-media-pipeline-upload-to-delivery)).
* The 2 orphaned-`brand_id` shoots that never reach the view → **DATA-FIX-001**.
* Any grid/layout/token change ([IPI-372](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-372/design-055b-shoots-list-react-parity-workspace-restart-v2-image-first), Done).

---

* `shoot_portfolio_view` columns include `cover_url` (text) + `start_date` (date) — `information_schema.columns`.
* View def derives cover: `CASE WHEN s.mood_board_urls IS NOT NULL AND cardinality(...) > 0 THEN s.mood_board_urls[1] ELSE NULL END AS cover_url`.
* App code probe: `grep -rc cover_image src/app/(operator)/app/shoots` → **0** (no broken select today, but no real cover either).
* Base data: 12 shoots → 10 owned; **8/10 have a moodboard cover**, 2 null.

Title: MOBILE-004 · Focus-Trap, Esc-to-Close, Streaming aria-live Region
Description:
## Context

`Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2/§16.3/§17: toasts already carry `role="status" aria-live="polite"` across 7 surfaces (done, design-side confirmed). Remaining, explicitly Claude-Code-owned: focus-trap on all modals/sheets (EvidenceBlock modal, BottomSheet, brand-switcher sheet, intelligence sheet), Escape-to-close, and a streaming `aria-live` region on the AI "thinking"/streaming text so screen readers hear it as it arrives (not just at the end). No existing Linear issue tracks this (confirmed via `docs/linear/issues/` search).

## Problem

Keyboard and screen-reader users can't reliably navigate modals/sheets (focus can escape to background content) and don't get live feedback while the AI response streams in.

## Proposal

1. Add a shared focus-trap behavior (trap Tab/Shift+Tab within the open modal/sheet, restore focus to the trigger element on close) to every modal/sheet component: EvidenceBlock modal, BottomSheet, brand-switcher sheet, intelligence sheet.
2. Wire Escape key to close the topmost open modal/sheet.
3. Add an `aria-live="polite"` (or `"assertive"` where appropriate) region around the AI chat-dock's streaming text so partial content is announced as it streams, not just the final message.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tab/Shift+Tab cannot move focus outside an open modal/sheet
- [ ] Focus returns to the triggering element when a modal/sheet closes
- [ ] Escape closes the topmost open modal/sheet
- [ ] Streaming AI response text is wrapped in an `aria-live` region; verified with a screen reader (VoiceOver/NVDA) or axe
- [ ] No regression to existing toast `aria-live` behavior (already done, do not duplicate/break)

## Skills

`accessibility` (focus-trap + live-region patterns) · `vercel-react-best-practices` — `rerender-use-ref-transient-values` (streaming text updates should not force full-tree re-renders — use a ref/imperative update for the visible text, only touch React state for the finalized message), `client-event-listeners` (dedupe the global Escape-key listener rather than attaching one per open modal)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `MOBILE-PLAN.md` §10, §16.3, §17
* Toasts' existing `aria-live="polite"` implementation (already shipped) — reuse the same pattern for the streaming region

## Out of scope

Full WCAG audit beyond these 3 items (touch targets tracked in MOBILE-002, long-press in MOBILE-003)
Creator: ai@socialmediaville.ca

Title: MOBILE-002 · Touch Target Enforcement (≥44px hit areas)
Description:
## Context

Design-side spec is done and verified: `Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2/§17 (mobile audit, 2026-06-30, re-confirmed 2026-07-01) found selection checkboxes render at 20–24px on Matching/Campaigns/Assets — below the 44×44px minimum touch target. The design source explicitly assigns this as **Claude Code owned**: "pad the hit area on mobile (React: `min-h-11 min-w-11`)." Confirmed via `docs/linear/issues/` search: **no existing Linear issue tracks this** despite being a named, specified gate item on the design side.

## Problem

Selection checkboxes and other small interactive controls (icon buttons, chips) may render below the accessible touch-target minimum on mobile breakpoints, especially in Matching/Campaigns/Assets selection UI.

## Proposal

Audit all interactive controls in `app/src/components/**` rendered at mobile breakpoints (<768px) and ensure a ≥44×44px hit area — the *visual* control can stay its current size; pad the tappable/clickable region (e.g. Tailwind `min-h-11 min-w-11` + centered visual glyph, or an invisible padding wrapper).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Identify every selection checkbox / icon-only control rendered on Matching, Campaigns, and Assets screens
- [ ] Each has a ≥44×44px tap target on mobile (verify via computed box size, not just CSS declaration)
- [ ] axe-core / Playwright a11y check added asserting minimum target size where feasible
- [ ] No visual regression on desktop (checkbox/icon visual size unchanged — only the tappable padding grows)
- [ ] Verified at 390px and 430px viewport widths

## Skills

`accessibility` (WCAG target-size guidance) · `vercel-react-best-practices` (keep the padding change CSS/Tailwind-only, no new JS-driven layout — avoid unnecessary re-renders per `rerender-*` rules)

## Dependencies

* Design spec: `Universal design prompt/MOBILE-PLAN.md` §16.2, §17 (source of truth for the requirement)
* Related, done: [IPI-264](https://linear.app/amo100/issue/IPI-264/design-mobile-verification-passfail-matrix-and-breakpoints) (DESIGN · Mobile Verification — Pass/Fail Matrix & Breakpoints)

## Out of scope

Long-press-to-select (tracked separately, see MOBILE-003) · tablet layout (see MOBILE-005)

---

# Implementation Prompt Pack (2026-06-30)

**Worktree:** `ipi/275-persistent-chat-dock` · `../wt-ipi-275-persistent-chat-dock`
**Skills:** copilotkit (v2) · mastra · frontend-design · shadcn · mermaid-diagrams · ipix-wireframe
**MCP:** CopilotKit MCP (docs) · Mastra docs server
**Design ref:** `Universal design prompt/components/` dock prototype · handoff §03 component map

## User stories

* As an **operator**, the dock is always visible at the workspace base with streaming responses — never a blank chat area.
* As an **operator on mobile**, the dock pins to the bottom and expands as a sheet without covering the active form.
* As a **developer**, there is exactly **one** dock component; routes pass context via `useAgentContext`, never fork the UI.

## Desktop wireframe

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
| workspace content (scrolls)                   |
+-----------------------------------------------+
| [*] production-planner . Spring Campaign      |  <- context strip (from IPI-197 hook)
| [Generate deliverables] [Plan shoot] [Explain]|  <- chips (from IPI-197 hook)
| +-------------------------------------------+ |
| | Ask anything...                      [up] | |  <- input + send
| +-------------------------------------------+ |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

## Mobile wireframe (<=1024px)

```
+----------------+   collapsed:
| content        |   | chat . Spring  [up] |  <- pinned bar
|                |   tap up -> expands to sheet (70vh)
+----------------+
| chat . Spring  |  <- dock pinned above BottomNav (IPI-251)
+----------------+
| [o][o][o][o]   |  <- BottomNavigation
+----------------+
```

## State diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Collapsed
    Collapsed --> Expanded: tap / focus
    Expanded --> Streaming: send message
    Streaming --> Expanded: response complete
    Streaming --> Error: stream fail
    Error --> Expanded: retry
    Expanded --> Collapsed: dismiss / route blur
```

## Message sequence (route-aware)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    actor Op as Operator
    participant Dock as PersistentChatDock
    participant CK as CopilotKit runtime
    participant M as Mastra agent
    Op->>Dock: type / click chip
    Dock->>CK: send + useAgentContext(brandId, shootId, selection)
    CK->>M: resolve agentId via route-agent-map (IPI-247)
    M-->>CK: stream tokens
    CK-->>Dock: render streaming
    Dock-->>Op: answer + follow-up chips
```

## Implementation steps (Test block each)

| Step | Prompt | Test / proof |
| -- | -- | -- |
| **A** | Create `app/src/components/persistent-chat-dock/` — single client component; mount in operator layout below workspace slot | renders on `/app/shoots` |
| **B** | Wire CopilotKit v2 (`/v2` imports) + `useAgent({ agentId })` resolved from route-agent-map (IPI-247) | agent connects, no "not found" |
| **C** | Integrate IPI-197 greeting/chip hooks (`useRouteWelcome` / `useConfigureSuggestions`) — **no duplicate strings here** | greeting names active brand/shoot |
| **D** | Streaming render + error/retry banner (states above) | stream + forced-error retry work |
| **E** | Mobile pin + expand sheet (<=1024px), above BottomNav; touch targets >=44px | Playwright mobile viewport pass |

## Out of scope

HITL approve UI (IPI-244) · per-agent tools (IPI-259/260/261) · thread persistence (license). Dock shell + context + streaming only. **Welcome/chip copy owned by IPI-197.**

## Verification

```bash
cd app && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run build && npm run test:e2e
```

Routes to test: `/app` · `/app/shoots` · `/app/shoots/new` · `/app/brand/[id]` (greeting changes per route).

## Evidence required (Done gate)

- [ ] Desktop + mobile screenshots (collapsed/expanded/streaming/error)
- [ ] Playwright report · task-verifier
- [ ] PR link · Linear → Done

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-SHOTS — Add, edit, and remove shot cards inline
Description:
## Purpose

Add, edit, and remove shot cards inline on `/app/shoots/[shootId]`. Separate from EDIT-BASIC (IPI-210) and EDIT-DELIVERABLES (IPI-216) per one-concern rule.

## User story

As an operator I can add, edit, and remove shots inline so that the shot list reflects the current plan.

## Schema facts (verified)

* `shoot.shot_list.id uuid primary key` — stable IDs exist
* `shoot.shot_list.status shoot.shot_status` = `pending | captured | approved` — already present
* No new status column needed for this task

## API routes

```
POST   /api/shoots/[shootId]/shots            → 201 { shot_id }
PATCH  /api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/[id]       → 200 { shot_id }
DELETE /api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/[id]       → 204
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
```

## Supabase

* `upsert_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_id uuid default null, p_description text, p_style_notes text default null, p_order integer default null)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = '' — schema-qualified: `shoot.shot_list`
* `delete_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_shot_id uuid)` — verify ownership before delete
* Audit: `agent_name: "operator-edit"` (non-blocking)

## Files

| File | Change |
| -- | -- |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-shot-cards.tsx` | New — editable shot cards |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/route.ts` | New — POST |
| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/shots/[id]/route.ts` | New — PATCH + DELETE |
| `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_shoot_shot_rpcs.sql` | upsert + delete RPCs |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. Add shot → new row in DB, appears in list
- [ ] B. Edit description/style_notes → DB updated
- [ ] C. Remove shot → row deleted
- [ ] D. 403 if not owner; 404 if shoot or shot missing
- [ ] E. Audit log per action (non-blocking)
- [ ] F. Shot order preserved after edit
- [ ] G. Button disabled while POST in flight

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-SHOTS for the iPix operator app.

Schema: shoot.shot_list.id is uuid primary key (stable IDs exist).
shoot.shot_list.status is shoot.shot_status enum (pending|captured|approved) — do not modify status in this task.

1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_shoot_shot_rpcs.sql
   upsert_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_id uuid default null, p_description text, p_style_notes text default null, p_order integer default null)
   - SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - IF p_id IS NULL: INSERT INTO shoot.shot_list(shoot_id, description, style_notes, "order")
   - ELSE: UPDATE shoot.shot_list SET description=p_description, style_notes=p_style_notes, "order"=COALESCE(p_order,"order") WHERE id=p_id AND shoot_id=p_shoot_id
   - RETURN shot_id

   delete_shoot_shot(p_shoot_id uuid, p_shot_id uuid)
   - SECURITY DEFINER; SET search_path = ''
   - DELETE FROM shoot.shot_list WHERE id=p_shot_id AND shoot_id=p_shoot_id

2. Create route.ts files for POST, PATCH, DELETE
   Auth: withOperatorAuth → brand check → svc.rpc(...)
   Audit (non-blocking)

3. Create shoot-shot-cards.tsx
   Editable cards: add card button, inline edit form, delete button
   Disable buttons while request in flight

Branch: ipi/217-shoot-detail-edit-shots
```

**Blocks:** IPI-213

Title: SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-BASIC — Edit shoot name, brief, channels, and budget inline
Description:
## Purpose

Edit name, brief, target channels, estimated budget, and budget breakdown on `/app/shoots/[shootId]` — one PATCH route, one RPC. Deliverables and shots are separate issues (IPI-216, IPI-217).

## User story

As an operator I can edit the shoot name, brief, channels, and budget inline on the Shoot Detail page so that I keep the plan accurate without re-running the wizard.

## What changes

* Edit toggle (pencil icon) on each field group; on save POST to API route
* Optimistic UI update; revert + toast on error

## API route

```
PATCH /api/shoots/[shootId]
Auth: withOperatorAuth → 401
Body: Partial<{ name, brief, target_channels, estimated_budget, budget_breakdown }>
Response: 200 { shoot_id } | 400 | 401 | 403 | 404 | 500
```

## Supabase

* RPC `update_shoot_draft(p_shoot_id uuid, p_name text default null, p_brief text default null, p_target_channels text[] default null, p_estimated_budget numeric default null, p_budget_breakdown jsonb default null)` — SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = '' — schema-qualified: `shoot.shoots`
* Audit: `ai_agent_logs` row `agent_name: "operator-edit"` — non-blocking (catch + log, never throw)

| `app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/route.ts` | New — PATCH handler |
| `app/src/components/shoots/shoot-edit-basic.tsx` | New — name/brief/channels/budget form |
| `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_update_shoot_draft_rpc.sql` | New |

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A. PATCH updates name/brief/channels/budget in DB
- [ ] B. Optimistic UI update on save; revert on error
- [ ] C. 400 on validation failure; 403 if access denied; 404 if shoot missing
- [ ] D. Audit log inserted (non-blocking)
- [ ] E. RPC uses SECURITY DEFINER + SET search_path = ''
- [ ] F. `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` clean

## Implementation prompt

```
Implement SHOOT-DETAIL-EDIT-BASIC for the iPix operator app.

Stack: Next.js 15 (app/), TypeScript strict, Supabase Postgres, Mastra agents.
Auth pattern: withOperatorAuth(req) → OperatorAuthError → 401
Brand check: createSupabaseServerClient() RLS — see app/src/app/api/shoots/commit/route.ts
Service role writes: createClient(url, SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) + svc.rpc(...)
Page pattern: app/src/app/(operator)/app/brand/[id]/page.tsx

Task:
1. Create supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_update_shoot_draft_rpc.sql
   - Function: update_shoot_draft(p_shoot_id uuid, p_name text default null, ...)

   - Only update non-null params

2. Create app/src/app/api/shoots/[shootId]/route.ts (PATCH handler)
```
